title: YouTube Niches Researcher (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
Hi, we run multiple YouTube channels in different niches. Some are growing fast, and some need more content ideas and better niche research.



Now we’re hiring a full-time Virtual Assistant to help us find new YouTube channels and content opportunities every day.



This role is for someone who is consistent, fast, and detail-oriented. You’ll be responsible for collecting large amounts of channel data, based on our criteria.



Who this job is perfect for:

-> you’re good at internet research and digging for info

-> you can work full-time and stay focused

-> you’re organized and accurate when handling data

-> you enjoy simple repetitive tasks

-> you want a stable, long-term remote job



What you’ll do:

-> search and collect YouTube channels from different niches

-> follow clear criteria and examples

-> submit daily research to our internal system

-> improve based on feedback

-> help us find new content opportunities every day



You’ll thrive here if you:

-> are consistent and self-motivated

-> enjoy structured work

-> can focus for long periods without distraction

-> want to grow with a company and prove yourself



Compensation & Perks:

-> $400-$700/month depending on skill, consistency, and results

-> Performance bonuses for finding good Channels/Niches (Daily EXTRA $$$)

-> 100% Remote: work from anywhere

-> Flexible hours option: as long as the job gets done

-> Long-term path to higher paying positions, such as team lead (manager)

Fill out this short Google Form (3–5 mins):



We’re hiring fast. If you’re ready to work hard and grow with a digital media company, apply now.

title: Founding SDR / Business Development Specialist
description:
Company: Ledger Lane

Employment Type: Full-time

Location: Remote



About Ledger Lane

Ledger Lane is a new accounting brand being launched to support businesses outside of the NDIS sector. Our goal is to diversify our services and work with growing businesses across industries such as retail, cafés, hospitality, and professional services.

We are building a high-performing Sales Development team to help launch and scale the business. This role is an opportunity to join as one of the founding tea
mbers and play a key role in shaping the growth strategy of the company.



About the Role

We are looking for a top-performing Sales Development Representative (SDR) who will help build the foundation of our sales pipeline. This is not a typical SDR role — you will help launch the brand, develop outreach strategies, build lead databases, and contribute to the overall business growth plan.

You will work closely with the leadership team to identify opportunities, create systems, and generate qualified leads for the business.



Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain a high-quality database of cold leads across target industries (retail, cafés, hospitality, professional services, etc.)

Develop and execute cold outreach strategies 
, LinkedIn, calls, and other channels)

Identify and qualify potential clients for Ledger Lane accounting services

Assist with launch strategy and go-to-market planning

Contribute ideas for website development, messaging, and positioning

Research target markets and identify business opportunities

Schedule and coordinate discovery calls with prospective clients

Maintain CRM systems and track outreach activity and results

Work closely with leadership to refine the sales process



Ideal Candidate

We are looking for someone who is proactive, strategic, and capable of helping build something from the ground up.

Preferred experience includes:

Sales Development Representative (SDR), Business Development, or Lead Generation roles

Experience working with or selling to retail, café, hospitality, or small business sectors

Familiarity with the accounting or financial services industry is a strong advantage

Experience with cold outreach, CRM systems, and database building

Strong research and prospecting skills

Ability to work independently and take initiative

Key Skills

Lead generation and prospecting

Cold outreach and sales communication

Strategic thinking and market research

CRM and sales pipeline management

Strong written and verbal communication

Self-motivated and results-driven



Why Join Ledger Lane?

Be part of a founding team launching a new brand

Opportunity to help build and shape the sales strategy

Long-term growth potential as the SDR team expands

Work directly with leadership on a high-impact project

title: Part-Time WordPress + Elementor VA for Fast Website Updates
description:
Job Description:

We are a growing trademark law firm looking for a reliable part-time VA who can help with small website updates inside WordPress using Elementor. Our site was recently moved from Divi to Elementor by our outside web/SEO team. They handle the bigger projects. We need someone who can step in for the smaller things so simple updates do not bottleneck the team.



This is not a full website designer role.

This is not a full developer role.

This is a practical, detail-heavy support role for someone who knows WordPress well, is comfortable in Elementor, and can make clean updates quickly.



What You’d Help With:

Make small edits to existing WordPress pages using Elementor

Update text, buttons, links, spacing, sections, and images

Add or remove sections from pages while keeping the design clean

Format blog posts or landing pages as needed

Help fix small mobile display issues or layout issues

Update basic page settings, forms, and simple styling elements

Follow clear directions from our team

Flag anything that looks broken, off-brand, or beyond your scope





What We’re Looking For:

Strong experience with WordPress

Experience using Elementor (or ability to learn it quickly)

Able to make clean, professional-looking page edits without breaking things

Good eye for layout, spacing, and visual consistency

Comfortable learning our site structure quickly

Able to follow instructions and also use judgment when something looks off

Strong English written communication

Detail-oriented and dependable

Able to work independently without needing constant hand-holding





Big Plus If You Also Have:

Experience supporting SEO teams with on-page updates

Familiarity with blog formatting and landing page updates

Basic understanding of responsive/mobile design

Experience with image sizing/compression

Familiarity with WordPress plugins, forms, and page builders in general





What Success Looks Like in This Role:

Small website edits get handled quickly and accurately

Our outside dev/SEO team stays focused on bigger projects

Our internal team does not get stuck trying to figure out Elementor

You help us keep the site clean, updated, and moving forward





Hours / Schedule:

Part-time to start

Likely around 5 to 10 hours per week to begin, with room to grow if it’s a great fit

Flexible schedule, but responsiveness and follow-through matter

title: Influencer & Creator Partnerships Manager (with US Market experience)
description:
We are seeking an enthusiastic, highly organized, and strategically-minded Influencer & Creator Partnerships Manager to lead our outreach and seeding efforts. This is a part-time, remote role (20 hours/week) designed for a specialist who doesn’t just execute tasks but brings high-level judgment to creator selection and content strategy.



We prioritize candidates who move beyond simple execution. You should have a deep "content sense"—the ability to understand what feels native to a platform, what resonates with an audience, and how creator activity drives genuine brand growth.



Work Structure & Requirements

Type: Part-time (20 hours/week).



Location: Remote.



Timezone: Must be available to work within New Zealand Standard Time (NZST) hours.



Exclusivity: Candidates must not have a concurrent full-time job to ensure dedicated focus.



Market Priority: Deep familiarity with the US or UK markets is essential. You must understand the specific consumer behaviors, platform trends, and cultural nuances of these regions.



Key Responsibilities

Strategic Research & Content Evaluation



Identify creators who align with a purpose-driven mission and high-end storytelling style.



Move beyond "database searching" to conduct deep evaluations of content quality, storytelling strength, and commercial potential.



Outreach & Partnership Execution



Execute highly personalized outreach to creators and talent managers.



Draft creative briefs that ensure content feels platform-native while staying aligned with the brand’s belief system.



Growth & Community Monitoring



Coordinate seeding logistics and monitor platforms for mentions and high-performing tags.



Use content intuition to identify new "waves" of partnership opportunities that drive both engagement and conversion.



Required Skills & Experience

Purpose-Driven Experience: Proven track record working with brands built around a clear mission or belief system (especially in Wellness, Personal Care, or Beauty).



Platform Mastery: Deep, hands-on expertise in at least one core platform (TikTok or Instagram). You should know how to produce or guide content that feels native and high-performing.



High-Level Judgment: While tools/AI can track data, we value your intuition. You must be able to judge what content will actually drive outcomes.



Market Expertise: Strong understanding of platform behaviors and consumer expectations specifically in the US or UK.



System Mindset: 5+ years of experience bringing structure to creator workflows and improving efficiency over time.



Commercial Awareness: A clear understanding of how creator storytelling connects to brand performance and conversion.



To Apply

Please submit your resume along with a Loom video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself.



Crucial: Please also include examples of past work (e.g., a content portfolio, specific campaign results, or platform-specific case studies) that demonstrate your ability to drive outcomes through content.

title: E - Commerce Outbound Sales Representative
description:
Overview

We are seeking a high-energy, execution-focused Outbound Sales Representative to join the team. Based in New York City, we are a "better-for-you" snack brand offering clean-label candied nuts and organic dried mangoes to major distributors and retail partners.



This is a pure execution role with high activity, clear metrics, and no fluff. You will be responsible for high-volume prospecting to engage cold leads, re-activate stalled accounts, and drive reorders from existing customers.



Work Structure & Requirements

Location: Remote (Must have a quiet, professional calling environment).

Schedule: Full-time, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM EST.

Requirement: Candidates must not have a concurrent full-time job to ensure total focus on hitting daily activity targets.

Tech Needs: Reliable high-speed internet and strong computer literacy to multitask across sales tools.



Key Responsibilities

Outbound Outreach: Execute high-volume outbound
s and daily cold calls to prospects and warm leads.

Account Management: Follow up with inactive accounts and call current customers to facilitate reorders, expansion, or reactivation.

Lead Response: Respond promptly to all inbound calls and
 inquiries.

CRM Discipline: Log all activity accurately in Salesforce, maintaining clean data and clear next steps for every lead.

Performance: Consistently hit daily activity targets and monthly pipeline goals.



Required Skills & Experience

Sales Background: Prior experience in outbound sales, inside sales, or lead generation.

Communication: Fluent English (reading, writing, and speaking) with the ability to write concise, non-spammy cold
s.

Phone Presence: Confident, natural delivery on the phone—no monotone script reading.

Resilience: Comfortable handling objections, basic discovery, and repetitive outreach.

Efficiency: Fast typing skills and high attention to detail.



Traits We Value

Metrics-Driven: Comfortable being measured by clear KPIs and pipeline creation.

Coachable: Open to feedback and a direct communicator who strictly follows the established sales process.

Reliable: Consistent and self-disciplined in a remote work setting.



To Apply

Please submit your resume along with a Loom video introducing yourself.

title: Motion Designer
description:
Job Type: Project-Based (40+ hours/month minimum, with strong potential for consistent ongoing work)

Rate: USD $8–$12/hour (based on experience)



About Us



MoveUp Studio is a motion design and digital content studio based in Auckland, New Zealand. We specialise in premium, highly custom animations for B2B SaaS and tech companies. Think product feature explainers, social cutdowns, paid ads, and event promos. Our work is bespoke, never templated. We care about quality and we're building something worth being part of.



Website: 



The Role



We're looking for a talented motion designer to join our small, collaborative team. To be upfront: this starts as project-based work, not a full-time position. We have an immediate project ready to go, with a minimum of 40 hours/month.

Here's what matters though. We're a growing studio actively scaling our client base. The right person who delivers great work and shows commitment will see that reflected in more consistent projects and a bigger role over time. We want to find someone who grows with us, not someone we have to keep rehiring for.



What You'll Be Doing



Creating motion graphics and animations in After Effects

Working from design files and storyboards to produce polished final animations

Exporting and optimising assets for web, social, and digital ad formats

Collaborating with our designers and production lead to hit deadlines and maintain quality

Contributing ideas and creative solutions, not just executing briefs



What We Need From You



Technical skills:



Strong proficiency in Adobe After Effects (non-negotiable)

Solid working knowledge of the broader Adobe Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, Premiere Pro)

Ability to export in multiple formats optimised for different platforms

Clean, organised project files



Who you are:



Trustworthy. We give you responsibility and expect you to own it.

A strong communicator. You flag issues early, ask questions when things aren't clear, and keep us updated without being chased.

Efficient. You manage your time well and deliver on deadline.

A fast learner. You pick up new styles, techniques, and feedback quickly.

A creative thinker and problem solver. You don't just follow instructions. You think about how to make the work better.



What We Offer



A real path to consistent work. We're not looking for a one-off freelancer. If you prove yourself on early projects, you'll be the first person we call as work grows. Our pipeline is building and we need reliable people we trust.

A collaborative remote culture. We're a small team that genuinely enjoys working together. You'll have a voice, not just a task list.

Creative projects that push you. We're always trying to create better work. You won't be churning out the same thing on repeat.

Flexibility and trust. We don't micromanage. We trust you to do great work and manage your own time. In return, we expect your best effort and strong communication.



How to Apply



Please send:



Your portfolio or showreel. We want to see your motion work specifically (not just static design).

A short message about why you think you'd be a good fit for MoveUp Studio. Be specific. Tell us what stood out to you about us and what you'd bring to the team.



Generic applications without a portfolio or a personalised message won't be considered.

We're excited to find the right person. If this sounds like you, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Proofreader / Copy Editor (Work from home opportunity)
description:
Job description

We are looking for a highly proficient proofreader/editor responsible for maintaining the quality of the SEO content we write for our range of clients. We prefer someone from a journalism and communications background, and the candidate must have an extremely high level of attention to detail. Experience writing for Australian audiences for an agency is a plus. We work across multiple different topic areas with strict deadlines.



Area Ten is a global digital agency that services large corporations to fast-growing upstarts across Australia, Asia and North America. One of the keys to our success lies in the quality of our staff. We have a strict policy of no junior staff which means you’ll be working with the highest calibre of colleagues in the industries, hence there is a scope for ample opportunities to learn and develop.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



·You will have to:



*Acquire and review content briefs, ensuring alignment with client requirements. Develop and manage brand guides and templates to maintain consistency across all content

*Perform end-to-end content editing, from line edits to comprehensive copy edits, ensuring all content meets Area Ten's high standards and client specifications

*Provide constructive feedback to copywriters, manage revisions, and update content based on client feedback to refine and perfect deliverables

*Work closely with a team of editors and content creators, sharing insights and strategies to enhance content quality and workflow efficiency

*Help manage our content database, which requires regular upkeep on the progress of content projects across multiple clients



REQUIREMENTS



*Strong background in content editing and proofreading, preferably within a digital agency setting.

*Exceptional attention to detail and a keen eye for grammatical and stylistic errors. Must be committed to following style guidelines such as the Australian Government Style Manual and the Associated Press Stylebook, 2019.

*Must have a high-speed internet connection and a laptop with high specifications.

*A degree in journalism, communications, or a related field is preferred.

*The resource will also have to experiment with various AI tools and produce quality SEO content for our partner clients. This is a full-time role and involves closely working with the other editors and the rest of the team.



Mandate Requirement: The candidate should have a high-speed internet connection and a laptop with high computer specifications.



Please note: You will have to complete 1 copy test and 1 proofreading exercise as a part of the interview process.



ELIGIBILITY:



· Proofreading: 2 years (Required)



· Copywriting: 5 years (Required)



HOW TO APPLY:



If you’re interested in a role with us, please answer this:



Job Type: Full-time, Permanent



Benefits:



Work from home

title: SEO Content Writer
description:
We are looking for a highly proficient full time SEO Content Writer.



Area Ten is a global digital agency that services large corporates to fast-growing upstarts across Australia, Asia and North America. We're an SEO agency that needs copy written for a range of clients covering an extensive range of industries/topics. One of the keys to our success lies in the quality of our staff. We have a strict policy of no junior staff which means you’ll be working with a high calibre of colleagues which presents ample opportunities to learn and develop. For a bit more context please feel free to view this video from the founder Jeremy Tang.



You will be expected to take briefs that define details like keywords and questions to use to build out into 900-word articles, highlight where you've used primary and related keywords in the content and add internal links to other pages (which we will provide you with).

Typically, we require 2 to 3 articles per day.

Fluent in written and verbal English

Experience writing for e-commerce

Experience writing for service businesses

Strong attention to detail

The ability to follow instructions and specific writing guidelines

Pedantic about spelling & grammar

Punctual and highly responsive

Demonstrated capability and experience writing across an extensive range of topics

To produce an absolute minimum of 1800 words per day worth of copy

Mandate Requirement: The candidate should have a high-speed internet connection and a laptop with high computer specifications.



We are seeking a dedicated candidate committed to working full-time with undivided focus and attention on this role, without engagement in external side hustles or concurrent employment commitments.





Please note: You will have to complete 2 copy tests as part of the interview process.



Job Type: Full-time

title: Sales Account Executive
description:
How to Apply

* Send your most updated resume to 



About us:



Gushwork.ai builds AI Agents that help small and medium businesses automate their entire inbound marketing stack — from SEO and website content to reviews, blogs, and lead funnels. Our current focus is the manufacturing and industrial distributors industry.



In plain English: we’re replacing bloated marketing agencies with intelligent, always-on software.



Backed by Lightspeed, SIG, Beenext, and B Capital, Gushwork.ai is one of the fastest-growing AI companies globally.



In just a year, we’ve grown to $1.5M ARR and are scaling fast toward $15M ARR in the next 12 months — helping thousands of small businesses grow smarter, not harder.



The Role:



We’re looking for a closer who thrives in fast-paced, high-velocity sales environments. You’ll own the full sales cycle from discovery to close for qualified meetings booked by our 10-person BDR team. This is a quota-carrying role where you’ll close 4-8 deals monthly, with deal cycles ranging from 1-3 calls.



Working Hours: US Shift (9:00 AM - 6:00 PM EST)

Work Mode: Remote



What You’ll Do:



- Run discovery calls with qualified prospects (OEMs, distributors, manufacturers) to uncover pain points around lead generation and digital presence

- Deliver compelling demos that connect our AI SEO solution to specific business outcomes and revenue impact

- Navigate buying committees within SMB organizations, typically engaging with Marketing Directors, Sales Leaders, and C-suite

- Close deals on 12-month contracts with ACV of $5k-$25k, meeting or exceeding monthly quota

- Manage pipeline with discipline—forecast accurately, maintain detailed CRM hygiene, and move deals forward with urgency

- Partner with Growth Managers post-sale to ensure smooth handoffs and identify expansion opportunities

- Iterate on sales methodology by sharing wins/losses and collaborating on improving conversion rates



What Success Looks Like:



- Month 1-2: Ramp on product, market, and sales process; close your first 2-3 deals

- Month 3+: Consistently hit/exceed quota of 4-8 closed deals monthly

- Ongoing: Maintain 30%+ demo-to-close conversion rate and sub-30 day sales cycles



You’re a Great Fit If You Have:



- 2-4 years of closing experience in B2B SaaS, MarTech, or digital services

- Proven track record hitting/exceeding quota in a high-velocity sales environment

- SMB sales chops—you know how to build urgency, handle objections, and close without lengthy procurement cycles

- Consultative selling skills—you diagnose before prescribing and can articulate ROI clearly

- Comfort with ambiguity—you’re joining an early-stage startup where adaptability matters

- CRM proficiency (Zoho, HubSpot, Salesforce, etc.) and strong process discipline

- Excellent English communication skills for engaging with global prospects

- Want to hustle like crazy to hit the numbers!



Bonus Points For:



- Experience selling to manufacturers, distributors, or industrial/B2B companies

- Background in SEO, content marketing, or lead generation solutions

- Track record in 1-3 call close environments

- Familiarity with AI-powered products

- Experience selling to US/international markets



What We Offer:



- Accelerators for over-quota performance—top performers earn significantly more

- Qualified pipeline delivered daily by our BDR team—focus on closing, not prospecting

- Fast feedback loops—short sales cycles mean you learn and earn quickly

- Career growth—as we scale, top performers move into senior AE or team lead roles

- Equity in a VC-backed rocketship

- Collaborative, high-performance culture where wins are celebrated and losses are learning 
nts

- Remote work



Our Sales Process



- BDR books qualified meeting ? hands off to you

- Call 1: Discovery (30-45 min) - understand business, pain points, buying process

- Call 2: Demo + Proposal (45-60 min) - tailored walkthrough, ROI discussion, pricing

- Call 3 (if needed): Negotiation/Close - handle final objections, terms, signatures

- Handoff to Growth Manager for onboarding



How to Apply

* Send your most updated resume to

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
***PLEASE ATTACHED YOUR RESUME LINK ON YOUR APPLICATION***



Our client is a U.S.-based digital media company dedicated to providing expert content and specialized tools that help users maximize credit card rewards and travel more efficiently. They operate as a small, high-energy, and passionate team focused on delivering high-value, accessible information to a rapidly growing community. The environment is fast-paced, with a heavy emphasis on content performance, website usability, and maintaining a consistent, trusted brand.

About the Role

We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to serve as the functional backbone of our reporting systems. This is an operational role focused on continuity and growth—you will maintain existing data infrastructure while building out reporting for an exciting new product launch.



Unlike a "greenfield" architect role, this position is for a "doer" who enjoys managing scheduled queries, troubleshooting pipelines, and ensuring leadership has the Tableau dashboards they need to make decisions. You will work closely with Marketing, Tech, and Operations to translate business questions into technical insights without needing to rebuild the foundational infrastructure.





Responsibilities





System Maintenance & Continuity: Run and maintain current data pipelines and reporting systems to ensure zero downtime for existing business units.

New Product Reporting: Build additional reporting layers on top of existing data pipelines to support upcoming product launches.

SQL & BigQuery Management: Write, debug, and optimize intermediate-to-advanced SQL queries. Manage scheduled and preset queries within BigQuery for maximum efficiency.

Dashboarding: Design, build, and iterate on Tableau dashboards, ensuring all visualizations are accurate, intuitive, and updated for stakeholders.

Pipeline Troubleshooting: Perform hands-on troubleshooting of data pipelines and refresh failures to maintain data integrity.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Marketing and Product teams to provide ad-hoc reporting and actionable insights for growth.



Competencies and Qualifications



Must-Have

5+ years of professional experience in Data Analysis.

Intermediate to Advanced SQL proficiency (querying, optimization, and debugging).

Hands-on experience with BigQuery and managing cloud-based data environments.

Proven track record of building and maintaining complex Tableau dashboards.

Demonstrated ability in Data Cleaning & Manipulation and troubleshooting data pipelines.



Nice-to-Have

Degree in Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related field.

Experience with HubSpot, Stripe, and GA4 (Google Analytics) data.

Familiarity with ETL workflow management and data modeling/schema design.

Tableau Desktop or Google Data Analytics certifications



Key Qualities

Collaborative: You thrive in a team environment and communicate technical "whys" clearly to non-technical peers.

Analytical & Detail-Oriented: You have a natural tendency to double-check data accuracy and hunt for anomalies before they reach a dashboard.

Inquisitive: You don’t just pull data; you ask questions to understand the "so what" behind the numbers.

Action-Oriented: You are a proactive problem-solver who fixes query inefficiencies or dashboard lags before being asked.

Long-Term Focus: We are looking for a candidate seeking a stable, long-term role where they can grow with the company.



What We Offer



100% Remote Work – Work from anywhere in the Philippines.

Career Growth Opportunities – Gain hands-on experience, and continuous learning.

Application Process

title: Appointment setter — AI & automation sales
description:
You’ll be calling business owners and decision-makers to introduce Think ALM’s automation and AI services and book qualified consultations for our sales team. This is a phone-first role, so you must be confident making volume calls, handling objections, and hitting daily targets. You will work Australian business hours (AEST/AEDT) from home.



What you will do



Call cold data daily and introduce Think ALM’s services to business owners



Qualify prospects and book confirmed appointments for our consultants



Follow up on leads and manage your pipeline using our CRM



Report daily activity and results to the team lead



What we are looking for



Strong spoken English — clear, confident, and professional



Previous experience in cold calling, telemarketing, or appointment setting



Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote environment



Reliable internet connection and a quiet working environment



Available to work Australian business hours (AEST/AEDT, Mon–Fri)



Bonus: Interest or background in tech, AI, or business automation



Pay (choose your structure)



Commission



25 AUD per qualified appointment



Additional bonus on closed sales



Unlimited earning potential



Best for high performers



Base wage



Monthly retainer (fixed amount discussed during application)



Reduced per-appointment bonus



Best for those seeking more stability



Both structures are performance-driven. We reward people who show up, work the phones, and deliver results. Please indicate which pay structure you prefer when you apply.



How to apply



Send a short voice memo or video (60–90 seconds) introducing yourself and explaining why you would be great in this role



Attach your CV



Tell us which pay structure you prefer (Commission or Base wage)



Applications without a voice or video introduction will not be considered, as we need to hear you before we invite you to an interview.

title: Short-Form Video Editor (Fully Flexible, $3,000-$5,000 / month )
description:
Paid Trial To Complete (Required to Apply, $150 if you pass it)

When submitting your application, include these 3 trial videos: https://exchange-life-community.notion.site/Editor-Application-Test-Brief-326f1855927780788969ede1d820b810?source=copy_link



NOTE: Applications without the trial will not be considered.



Role Overview



We are looking for a highly creative and results-driven Performance Video Editor to join our team. Your mission will be to create high-performing organic video for Instagram and TikTok that don't look like ads. If you have a knack for creating content that stops the scroll and engages audiences, we want to talk to you.



This is a long-term, fully remote position for an editor who is passionate about the intersection of creativity and data, and is excited by the opportunity to have their work directly impact business results.



What You'll Do:



- Edit raw footage into compelling, fast-paced short-form video tailored for organic TikToks and Instagram Reels.

- Incorporate viral trends, engaging hooks, and retention-focused pacing to maximize performance.

- Add dynamic captions, relevant B-roll, and sound design to create a native viewing experience.

- Collaborate with our marketing team to test new creative concepts and iterate on winning ads to scale campaigns.

- Analyze ad performance to understand what resonates with the audience and apply those insights to future edits.



Who We're Looking For:



- A Viral Content Creator: You have proven experience creating organic short-form videos (TikToks, Reels) that have achieved significant reach and engagement. You understand what makes content shareable and how to capture attention in the first three seconds.

- A Performance-Minded Editor: You are excited by the idea of your creative work driving measurable results. Experience editing direct-response or paid social ads is a major plus.

- CapCut or Premiere Pro Whiz: You are highly proficient in a fast-paced editing workflow using modern tools.

- Excellent Communicator: You have strong English skills and are reliable, detail-oriented, and can work independently.

- Available & Ready: You have a perfect internet connection and are available to dedicate 3-4 hours daily to this role.

- This is a part-time remote role requiring approximately 24 hours per week (around 4 hours per day, Monday to Saturday), with the potential to grow into a full-time position based on performance.



Compensation & Growth: Earn Based on Performance



We believe in rewarding top talent for outstanding results. This role offers a unique compensation structure designed to give you significant earning potential.



- Base Rate: You will start with a competitive base rate of $10 per hour (596 PHP / hour)

- Performance Bonuses: On top of your hourly rate, you will earn substantial monthly bonuses for creating viral videos and "winning" ads that meet or exceed performance targets. Our bonus structure is uncapped.

- Earning Potential: Our top-performing editors consistently earn $3,000 - $5,000+ per month by creating ads that convert.



This is not a gig. It is a long-term partnership. Most of our editors have been with us for over a year, and we are committed to investing in your growth and success.



Why Join Our Team?



- Fully Remote: Work from anywhere in the world.

- Flexible Schedule: Manage your own hours and workflow.

- Direct Impact: See your creative work translate directly into business success and your own earnings.

- Growth & Stability: Join a team that values long-term relationships and provides ongoing training and opportunities.

title: Meta Ads Campaign Manager (Part time)
description:
About Us

We’re a programming education company that helps experienced software engineers build real-world Rust projects and land Rust roles. We run a high-ticket, cohort-based accelerator plus self-paced training, and reach engineers through YouTube,
, and partnerships.



Role Overview

We are looking for a part-time Meta Ads Operator to implement and maintain our
 and Instagram campaigns. This is an execution-focused role: you will take clear targeting and creative direction from the founder and marketing team, then handle setup, tracking, and ongoing optimizations inside Meta Ads Manager.



Ideal for someone who enjoys clean accounts, accurate tracking, and making data-driven tweaks, not sitting in branding meetings.



Key Responsibilities

1. Implement and maintain Meta Pixel and Conversion API across our funnels (waitlist, applications, bookings, purchases).

2. Set up campaigns, ad sets, and ads according to provided specs (objectives, budgets, audiences, and creatives).

3. Create and manage custom and lookalike audiences from our traffic,
 list, and engaged users.

4. Monitor performance daily on weekdays and execute simple rules: pause obvious losers, keep winners within defined guardrails, and flag issues quickly.

5. Run basic A/B tests on creatives and audiences following our testing framework.

6. Ensure tracking events fire correctly on all key steps and troubleshoot when they do not.

7. Produce a concise weekly report (spend, cost per waitlist opt-in, cost per application, cost per call, cost per student) with 2 to 3 clear recommendations.



You Are A Good Fit If

1. You have 1 to 3 years of hands-on experience running Meta Ads (lead gen or info/education experience is a plus).

2. You are comfortable with Meta Ads Manager, Pixel, standard events, and basic CAPI.

3. You can read numbers and make simple performance-based decisions without drama.

4. You are organized, responsive, and document what you do so others can follow it.

5. You can overlap at least 2 to 3 hours per day with US time zones.

6. You’re currently freelancing or working part-time and want a long term core client, not just a one-off project.



This Role Is Not

1. Not a creative director or copywriter (we have a content and
 team).

2. Not a CMO or growth strategist d
 offers, funnels, or pricing.

3. Not a general social media manager (no organic posting, community management, or influencer work).



Scope & Engagement

1. Part-time contractor.

2. We’ll start at 5–10 hours/week for the first 60–90 days while we validate and scale Meta.

3. We guarantee a minimum of 20 hours/month, paid monthly, so your time is predictable.

4. If performance is strong and we scale ad spend, this role can grow into 15–20+ hours/week and potentially a full-time PH position.

5. Remote, flexible schedule as long as SLAs are met and campaigns are monitored on weekdays.



How To Apply



Please reply to this job post with the following:

1. A brief summary of your Meta Ads experience, including typical monthly budgets.

2. Screenshots or summaries of 2 to 3 campaigns you have managed (goal, budget, structure, and results).

3. One paragraph on how you normally set up tracking (pixel, events, CAPI) for a lead gen funnel.

4. Your part-time hourly rate and full-time monthly rate, respectively.

title: Sales Manager
description:
Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced Sales Manager to lead and scale EVORETRO’s B2B distributor and retailer sales efforts, with a strong emphasis on retail partnerships and B2C-driven growth strategies. This role owns the end-to-end sales strategy across distributors, retailers, and direct-to-consumer–influenced channels, while managing and developing the sales team.

The Sales Manager will be responsible for driving revenue growth through retailer acquisition, promotional strategy, product bundling, and data-driven sales forecasting, while ensuring strong client relationships and operational excellence. The ideal candidate brings 3–5 years of sales experience, prior people-management exposure, and hands-on experience managing Shopify-based retail and wholesale environments.



Key Responsibilities:

Sales Strategy & Leadership

Own and execute the B2B sales strategy for distributors and retailers, with a strong understanding of B2C buying behavior and retail sell-through

Develop and manage retailer-focused sales strategies, including promotional calendars, product launches, and seasonal campaigns

Lead, coach, and manage the sales team, setting targets, monitoring performance, and driving accountability

Identify high-potential markets, retailers, and distributors across regions

Act as a senior point of contact for key retailer and distributor accounts

Drive structured outreach, follow-ups, and pipeline reviews to ensure consistent revenue growth

Business Development & Partnerships

Research, qualify, and onboard new retailers and distributors aligned with EVORETRO’s premium brand positioning

Lead negotiations covering pricing, payment terms, MOQs, promotional support, and merchandising agreements

Design and execute promotional strategies and product bundling initiatives to increase order value and sell-through

Present business cases, sales forecasts, and market demand insights to leadership

Represent EVORETRO at trade shows, conventions, and industry events (virtual or in-person)

Sales Operations & Team Management

Oversee sales operations across retailer and distributor channels, including:

Order accuracy and fulfillment coordination

Pricing and promotion compliance

Contract and commercial term alignment

Manage and optimize Shopify retail and wholesale sales channels, including retailer access, pricing tiers, and order workflows

Ensure CRM discipline and visibility across the sales team, including:

Lead and account status

Conversion rates

Forecasted revenue and pipeline health

Client & Account Management

Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key retailer and distributor partners

Serve as a trusted advisor to clients by understanding their sales goals, challenges, and merchandising needs

Ensure a high-quality partner experience from onboarding through ongoing account management

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Work closely with Product, Marketing, Finance, and Logistics teams to ensure:

Effective promotional execution and product bundling

Accurate pricing, margin, and cost alignment

Smooth order fulfillment and inventory planning

Share retailer and market feedback to support product development and pricing optimization

Performance Tracking, Forecasting & Analysis

Define, track, and report on key sales metrics, including:

Pipeline growth and velocity

Retailer and distributor conversion rates

Sales revenue, order volume, and average order value

Lead sales forecasting and performance analysis to support inventory planning and leadership decision-making

Deliver regular performance reports with insights, risks, and strategic recommendations

Continuously refine sales and promotional strategies based on data and results



Qualifications:

3–5 years of experience in B2B sales, retail sales, business development, or account management

Proven experience managing and leading a sales team

Strong background in retailer sales, with an understanding of B2C-driven growth strategies

Demonstrated success closing distributor, wholesaler, or retail partnerships

Experience managing Shopify-based retail and wholesale platforms

Strong negotiation, presentation, and client relationship management skills

Experience working with distributors, retailers, or international accounts

Familiarity with collectibles, trading cards, toys, or retail distribution is a strong advantage

Proficient in CRMs, spreadsheets, and productivity tools (Google Workspace, Excel)



Key Competencies:

Strategic, results-driven sales leadership

Strong sales strategy and promotional planning expertise

Product bundling and merchandising mindset

Excellent client and stakeholder management

Data-driven approach to forecasting and sales analysis

Highly organized with strong execution and follow-through

title: Product Development Manager
description:
Position Overview:

The Product Development Manager plays a central role in managing distributor relationships and leading product development initiatives across various markets. This role ensures the seamless execution of projects from ideation to delivery, maintaining high standards for product quality, project timelines, and profitability. The manager also spearheads private label programs, optimizes e-commerce platforms, and supports cross-functional collaboration with sales, marketing, logistics, and supply chain teams to drive operational success.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Manage and maintain distributor accounts via
 and scheduled meetings.

Oversee full-cycle product development for new and existing items, ensuring quality, profitability, and timely delivery.

Lead private label product development, including design, costing, supplier coordination, and timeline execution.

Coordinate with suppliers, factories, and internal departments to execute purchase orders and monitor production timelines.

Communicate order statuses and shipping updates to clients.

Investigate and resolve client quality issues through factory coordination and timely feedback.

Manage Rare Candy’s product listings, pricing, and customer service inquiries.

Develop and maintain sales tracking tools to monitor performance and listing effectiveness.

Supervise and support the sales department in targeting clients and preparing product specifications.

Guide the sales team in building strong business relationships with professionalism and strategic intent.

Collaborate with the marketing, design, and Shopify teams for successful product launches and branding.

Source, evaluate, and manage supplier relationships to ensure favorable terms and consistent quality.

Ensure accurate product information, photo assets, and listings across platforms and departments.

Qualifications:

Education & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Product Development, or a related field.

Minimum of 3–5 years experience in product development, account management, or a similar role.

Proven experience in managing cross-functional teams and projects in a product-driven business.

 

Skills & Competencies

Strong project management and organizational skills.

Excellent written and verbal communication abilities.

Strategic thinker with an eye for detail and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

Ability to handle multiple projects and deadlines while maintaining high-quality standards.

Skilled in supplier negotiation and relationship management, especially in global markets.

Strong analytical skills with the ability to use data to drive decisions and improvements.

Experience in handling e-commerce platforms and product listing management is a plus.



Preferred:

Experience working with international suppliers and distributors (e.g., U.S., Europe, Middle East).

Familiarity with private label development and quality assurance protocols.

Proficiency in Canva, Shopify, and project management platforms.

 

Collaboration & Reporting

Product Development Team – For managing timelines, product quality, and execution of new and existing items.

Sales Team – To align on client requirements, develop product specifications, and support strategic outreach efforts.

Marketing Team – To coordinate product launches, promotional materials, and ensure brand consistency.

Design Team – For packaging layouts, 3D mockups, and product visuals that meet branding and client expectations.

Shopify & E-commerce Team – To ensure accurate product listings, pricing updates, and availability of photo assets.

Supply Chain Team – To coordinate production schedules, monitor factory output, and ensure integration with inventory systems.

Warehouse & Logistics Team (China) – For shipping coordination, order fulfillment, and inventory tracking.

Suppliers & Manufacturers – To manage production, negotiate terms, resolve quality concerns, and ensure timely delivery.

title: AI Trainer

title: Expert Short-Term Rental Guest Support (US Evening/Night Shift)
description:
Hours: US evening/night shift (approximately 5PM–1AM Central Time USA). Full-time, 6 days per week.

Pay: $20-50k PHP monthly depending on experience and performance. Room to grow.

About Us:

We operate 21+ short-term rental properties across. Our guests book through Airbnb and VRBO, and we pride ourselves on fast, friendly communication and smooth stays. We're a small, growing team and we need someone reliable to own the overnight guest experience.

What You'll Do:



Monitor and respond to guest messages across Airbnb/VRBO via Hospitable (our property management platform)

Handle check-in/check-out questions, lockout issues, Wi-Fi troubleshooting, and common guest concerns

Coordinate with cleaners and runners through Breezeway and Slack when issues come up during your shift

Escalate maintenance emergencies following our written SOPs — gather information first, don't just forward problems

Complete shift handoff notes in Slack so the day team knows exactly where things stand

Monitor noise alerts and security cameras and respond per our protocols



What We're Looking For:



Prior experience in hospitality, property management, Airbnb co-hosting, or live chat customer support (strongly preferred)

Comfortable with: Slack, ClickUp, Hospitable (or similar PMS), and learning new tools quickly

Strong written English — you'll be writing directly to guests and your tone matters

Reliable. This is non-negotiable. You are covering the overnight shift alone. If you go offline, our guests have no one. We track active hours

Problem-solver who can follow SOPs but also think through situations independently

Stable internet connection and backup power plan



How to Apply:

Send us a message with:



A brief summary of your relevant experience (hospitality, STR, or live chat support)



Your available start date

A Loom video (under 5 minutes) responding to the scenario below. Don't script it — just talk through how you'd handle the situation naturally. We want to hear how you think, not a rehearsed answer.



Scenario:

It's 10 PM. You're the only person on shift for a company that manages 21 short-term rental properties. Three things happen within 5 minutes of each other:



A guest checking in tonight messages that their door code isn't working and they're standing outside with luggage.

A current guest at a different property sends a message complaining that the Wi-Fi has been down for an hour.

You notice a new booking request that expires in 3 hours. Nobody has responded to it yet.



In your video, walk us through: What do you do first, second, and third? Why that order? And what would you actually say to each guest?

Applications without the Loom video will not be reviewed.



Huge bonus if you have experience with claims 

Huge bonus if you worked higher up at airbnb

title: Operations Manager – Property Maintenance & Renovations (Sydney)
description:
SALARY: AUD $2,500/month (approx. PHP 105,000)

This is a senior full-time remote role. We pay well because we hire well.



ABOUT PROPERTYWORX SYDNEY:



Propertyworx Sydney is a property maintenance and multi-trade renovations company based in Australia. We work with property managers looking after residential portfolios, as well as private clients, across maintenance, repairs, and renovations.

We have a solid team, strong demand, and great clients. But right now, the founder is carrying all of operations — overseeing 30–60 active jobs, keeping the team accountable, answering every question, and solving every problem.

That needs to change. We are ready to bring in the right person to own operations completely, so our founder can step into sales and growth.



THE ROLE:



You will step directly into the founder's current operational role. That means taking full ownership of a busy job pipeline, managing a team of 4–6 people — including office staff, VA's, field tradespeople, and subcontractors — and being the go-to person when anything needs an answer or a fix.



You will work alongside our existing EA, but your scope and responsibility will be significantly broader. This is not a support role. This is a leadership role.



Working hours: 9:00am – 5:30pm AEST, Monday–Friday. Philippine-based candidates are welcome — you must be available during these hours.



WHAT YOU WILL OWN:



- The full job pipeline from inquiry to invoice — nothing stalls, nothing gets forgotten

- Day-to-day management of office staff, field tradespeople, subcontractors, and our VA

- Client-facing communication — handling calls and
s from property managers and private clients professionally and promptly

- Holding the team accountable to KPIs and deadlines without micromanaging

- Identifying broken or inconsistent processes and fixing them — we have SOPs, but they are not always followed consistently

- Building and documenting new workflows so the business runs without the founder in the room

- Managing ServiceM8 and Xero — recommending automations and improvements where useful





HOW WE WILL MEASURE YOUR SUCCESS

- Jobs created in ServiceM8 within 1 hour of inquiry

- Weekly KPI report delivered every Monday with issues flagged

- Zero stalled jobs without a documented reason

- Founder spending less than 20% of their time on operations within 90 days





REQUIREMENTS

You must have:



- 5+ years of PROVEN experience in an operations management, or similar leadership role

- Experience in property, trades, real estate, construction, or a comparable field-service industry (preferred but not essential — strong operators from similar industries will be considered)

- Proficiency with job management software — ServiceM8 strongly preferred, or equivalent such as Simpro, Tradify, or ServiceTitan

- Working knowledge of Xero or a comparable accounting platform

- Confident professional English — you will be speaking with Australian property managers, private clients, and tea
mbers regularly

- Available 9:00am–5:30pm AEST, Monday–Friday

- Reliable internet and a professional home office setup





THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF...



- You need months of hand-holding — we expect you to find your feet and be largely independent within four weeks

- You get overwhelmed easily or burn out under a consistently busy workload

- You wait to be told what to do instead of spotting what needs fixing and acting

- You struggle to hold yourself or others accountable





YOU ARE EXACTLY WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR IF...



You are a natural leader who takes full ownership and does not pass the buck

You can walk into a busy operation, understand how it works, and start improving it fast

You hold yourself and others to a high standard — calmly and consistently

You are resilient and composed under pressure, and energised by solving problems

You want a role with real responsibility and autonomy, not just a task list





WHY JOIN PROPERTYWORX:



- AUD $2,500/month — strong, stable salary for a full-time remote role

- Salary growth tied to business growth — as we scale, you scale with us

- 3-month probation, then a long-term permanent role with a business that is not going anywhere

- Real leadership position — your decisions will directly shape how this business runs

- Work directly with the founder from day one with genuine autonomy

- Join a growing business with loyal property management and private clients, and strong ongoing demand





HOW TO APPLY

We will only review applications that include all three of the following. Missing any component means your application will not be considered.



1. Your CV — highlight any operations, property, trades, or field-service experience

2. A Loom video (2–3 minutes) — introduce yourself and tell us about a time you took ownership of a broken process and fixed it. What was the situation, what did you do, and what was the result?

3. A written answer (200 words max) — tell us about a time someone on your team was underperforming or not following process. How did you handle it, and what was the outcome?



We read every application personally. If you are the right fit, we will be in touch within 5 business days.

title: Virtual Sales Closer / B2B Sales Rep (US Market) – Base + Commission
description:
We are a marketing agency looking for a remote sales rep / closer to speak with local business owners in the USA.



You do not need previous sales experience, but it is preferred. The most important things for this role are:

- great/excellent English

- confidence speaking with prospects

- comfort being on camera

- professionalism and coachability



This role has a lot of room for growth. You will receive training and support to improve your sales skills, perform at a higher level, and increase your earnings over time. This is a very lucrative opportunity for the right person.



You will be talking to business owners about our marketing services, so you must be able to communicate clearly, build trust, and speak confidently on video calls.



Responsibilities:

- Speak with leads and prospects from US local businesses

- Join discovery and sales calls

- Communicate our marketing services clearly and confidently

- Build rapport with prospects

- Answer questions and handle objections professionally

- Follow up with leads when needed

- Keep notes and pipeline updated in the CRM

- Work hours that overlap with the US market



Must-have requirements:

- Great/excellent spoken and written English

- Must have a webcam

- Must be comfortable speaking on video calls with prospects

- Professional communication skills

- Reliable internet connection

- Quiet workspace for calls

- Organized, dependable, and coachable



Preferred:

- Sales experience

- B2B experience

- Experience speaking with US customers

- Familiarity with marketing or agency services

- Familiarity with CRMs like HubSpot, GoHighLevel, Pipedrive, or similar



Compensation:

- Base pay + commission

- OTE: $2,000–$3,000/month

- Pay depends on communication skills, experience, and performance



How to apply:

To apply, you must do ALL of the following:



1. Submit a voice recording or video recording that is 60 seconds maximum (Video submission preferred)

3. In the recording, include the following:

   - Todays Date (Recording date)

   - your past experience

   - why you feel you are a good fit for this role

4. Include the word "Orange" in your application titles submission

5. Include your whatsapp contact



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.



This is not a customer service or general VA role. This is a sales role for someone who is confident speaking live with prospects and helping move conversations toward a sale.



We are looking for someone who is professional, hungry, confident, and great at communicating. If you have excellent English and are comfortable on camera, we would like to hear from you.

We are looking for a "Growth Partner" someone who doesn't just "tick and flick" ads, but someone who is obsessed with data, ROAS, and creative performance. You will be responsible for scaling our existing brands and launching our new brand, from $0 to $25k+.

mber here who is excited about data and media buying. If you have what it wakes to do the Job please mention ROAS at the start of your application to be considered.

title: Senior Amazon Marketplace Operator — Full Account Management, Brand Authority & Walmart
description:
MontBlack Inc. is a U.S.-based digital distribution company. We partner with legacy and family-owned consumer brands and manage their full marketplace presence on Amazon and Walmart. We currently operate 6+ active brand accounts and are bringing our marketplace operations fully in-house.

We are looking for someone who has operated inside an Amazon agency or multi-brand environment — someone who has seen and solved every type of Amazon problem across multiple accounts simultaneously.

You are not here to learn marketplace basics. You are here to install a system that scales.

Your responsibility is to build and maintain the marketplace operating system that allows MontBlack to grow multiple brands simultaneously. Walmart follows Amazon’s core logic — if you own Amazon PPC end-to-end, you will own Walmart within 30 days.



What You Will Own

PPC & Advertising

? Build and manage Amazon SP, SB, and SD campaigns across all brand accounts

? Build and manage Walmart Sponsored Ads inside Seller Center — candidates with strong Amazon PPC fundamentals will adapt quickly as Walmart’s ad structure follows similar logic

? Create SOPs for campaign structure, keyword strategy, bid management, and launch sequencing

? Build standardized product launch frameworks (discovery ? ranking ? scaling)

? Weekly search term audits, negative keyword management, and budget pacing

? Monitor and optimize ACoS, TACoS, CTR, CVR across the full portfolio

? Diagnose conversion issues impacting ad performance and align PPC with listing quality

? Build standardized reporting across all brands

? Actively use AI tools for keyword research, campaign analysis, and workflow efficiency — this is a requirement, not a bonus



Catalog & Listing Integrity

? Resolve suppressed listings, stranded inventory, broken variations, and catalog errors

? Build and manage product variations using flat files

? Fix catalog structures, merge duplicate ASINs, and correct listing attribution

? Reclaim listings under incorrect brand names or third-party seller accounts

? Manage bulk listing updates across multiple accounts

? Build SOPs for flat files, variation creation, and catalog maintenance



Brand Registry & Brand Authority

? Enroll brands in Amazon Brand Registry, including brands with pending or no active trademarks

? Execute Brand Registry through IP Accelerator and alternative pathways

? Identify and resolve listings attributed to incorrect or outdated brand names

? Execute brand name correction requests through Seller Support and Brand Registry

? Transfer listing ownership and brand attribution to the correct entity

? Manage the full reclamation process — from case opening to confirmed ownership transfer

? Handle brand entity consolidation and brand name corrections

? Manage storefronts and A+ Content coordination

? Execute hijacker removal, unauthorized seller reporting, and IP complaint filing

? Maintain a portfolio-level tracker for trademark status, Brand Registry, and protection



Walmart Operations

? Set up and manage Walmart Seller Center accounts for new brands

? Handle item setup, content score optimization, and catalog management

? Run and optimize Walmart Sponsored Products campaigns — if you own Amazon PPC, you will own this within 30 days

? Resolve Walmart-specific listing and catalog issues

? Build SOPs for Walmart onboarding and account management



What the First 90 Days Look Like

Days 1–30

? Full audit of all brand accounts (PPC, catalog, Brand Registry, hijackers, Walmart gaps)

? Deliver a written assessment with prioritized actions

Days 31–60

? Resolve suppressed listings

? Brand Registry enrolled or in progress for all brands

? PPC structures rebuilt or validated

? Walmart onboarding and advertising initiated

Days 61–90

? Full SOP library completed (PPC, catalog, onboarding, brand protection)

? Reporting system implemented

? Train at least one junior tea
mber on a core workflow



Performance Metrics

Performance will be evaluated based on:

? TACoS improvement across brands

? Speed of resolving suppressed listings and catalog issues

? Brand Registry enrollment completion rate

? Successful SOP system installation and team adoption



You Are the Right Fit If

? You have 4+ years in Amazon marketplace operations (agency or multi-account environment)

? You have managed multiple accounts simultaneously (8+ preferred)

? You have enrolled brands in Brand Registry, including without active trademarks

? You understand IP Accelerator and alternative enrollment pathways

? You have reclaimed listings and handled complex case escalations

? You have executed brand name corrections and listing ownership transfers

? You have basic Walmart Seller Center experience or have actively used it — deep Amazon mastery is the priority, but you must be willing to own Walmart fully within 30 days

? You have handled hijacker removal and IP complaints

? You are advanced with flat files, variations, and catalog fixes

? You actively use AI tools in your daily workflow — not occasionally, but as a core part of how you operate

? You have built SOPs and operational systems

? You think like an operator, not a task executor



Requirements

? Stable internet and professional home office

? Partial overlap with U.S. Eastern or Central time zones

? Available to start within 2–3 weeks

? Work samples required (SOP, flat file, campaign structure, or case outcome)



What We Offer

? $1,200 – $1,600/month based on experience

? Performance bonuses tied to portfolio growth and system delivery — top performers have a clear path to $2,000+/month as MontBlack scales

? Direct access to leadership — no micromanagement

? Remote, flexible work

? Clear growth path as MontBlack scales to 20+ brands

? A role built on operational discipline and systems ownership — if you take pride in running a clean, tight operation across multiple brands, this is the right environment



How to Apply

Use your favorite season as the subject line and include:

? Number of Amazon accounts managed simultaneously (and context: agency, in-house, freelance)

? One Brand Registry or listing reclamation case (problem + outcome)

? Experience with brands without active trademarks

? One example of a brand name correction or listing ownership transfer you executed

? Walmart Seller Center experience — if limited, explain how you would own it within 30 days

? Best ACoS or TACoS result (include spend and category)

? AI tools you use daily and how they improve your workflow

? Tools used daily (PPC, catalog, keyword research)

? Computer setup and number of monitors



Technical Question (Required)

Describe the difference between:

? Auto campaign keyword discovery

? Phrase match expansion

? Exact match scaling

And explain how you d
 when to move a keyword between them.



Upload Required

? Resume / CV (PDF)

? Internet speed screenshot

? One SOP, flat file template, or process document you created



Voice Recording (20–30 seconds)

Answer this out loud:

“A brand we represent has no registered trademark yet, their listings are attributed to the wrong brand name, a third-party seller has taken over their main ASIN, and the brand name on the listing belongs to a different entity we need to reclaim. Walk me through how you would approach fixing all of this.”

End your recording with:

“MontBlack protects legacy.”



Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed.

title: Medical Specialist Legal VA, personal injury
description: We are seeking a detail-oriented Medical Records Retrieval Specialist to assist our personal injury law firm in obtaining medical records. should know chart swap, MRO, Datavant.  Clio knowledge. medical record reductions, lien reductions.  The ideal candidate will have experience in efficiently managing requests and navigating the healthcare system to secure necessary documentation. sending clients to treatment and monitoring there treat in relation to policy limits a must. Strong communication skills and attention to detail are essential for this role. Familiarity with legal processes related to personal injury cases is preferred. If you're organized and motivated to support our legal team, we would love to hear from you!

title: CA Supervisor
description:
Company Overview



Join the leader transforming healthcare waste management across America.



MedPro Disposal is the nation's largest privately-held medical waste management company, serving 35,000+ providers across 48 states in a $2+ billion market. Since 2009, we've been the trusted partner helping healthcare facilities save up to 30% on waste management costs while maintaining 99% on-time service.



What we do: We lead with comprehensive medical waste disposal solutions, plus pharmaceutical waste management, secure document destruction, and compliance training services—all powered by innovative technology and a client-first approach.



Why it matters: We handle the critical but complex challenge of medical waste management so healthcare providers can focus on what they do best: delivering exceptional patient care.



Our impact: From physician practices to hospitals, dental offices to long-term care facilities, we're trusted advisors and strategic partners—not just vendors. We combine proven expertise with environmental responsibility and comprehensive protection to solve complex operational challenges.



Ready to grow with us?

Join a fast-scaling, mission-driven company where we Do The Right Thing, roll up our sleeves with a "figure it out" attitude, and win as a team. We're building something meaningful—making healthcare safer, more compliant, and more efficient—one client at a time.



Position Overview:

This role oversees day-to-day team performance, ensuring service excellence, operational efficiency, and adherence to established SLAs and KPIs.



The ideal candidate is an experienced operations professional with a proven track record in customer service leadership—capable of driving performance, coaching teams, and executing scalable processes that enhance customer satisfaction and retention.



This role will work closely with leadership, account managers, and cross-functional teams to deliver consistent, high-quality service and drive operational impact through continuous improvement.



Key Responsibilities:



Operations Management

• Oversee daily operations of customer service teams across all client accounts

• Ensure adherence to SLAs, KPIs, and performance standards across multiple brands and contracts

• Monitor and optimize operational efficiency, productivity, and quality metrics

• Implement and maintain operational processes, policies, and procedures

• Manage or support workforce planning (scheduling, queue coverage, workload distribution, and cross-training) based on experience level

• Support implementation of new tools, software, and systems



Team Leadership & Development

• Lead, mentor, and develop tea
mbers, including representatives, team leads, and supervisors

• Drive or support recruitment, onboarding, and retention strategies in partnership with recruitment

• Conduct performance evaluations and provide coaching for continuous improvement

• Foster a positive, accountable, and performance-driven work culture

• Address workplace concerns and escalate risks or issues to senior leadership as needed



Client Management

• Serve as an escalation point for client issues and concerns (scope dependent on experience)

• Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders

• Support or lead client reporting, business reviews, and performance discussions

• Identify opportunities for service improvement, client satisfaction, and account growth



Quality & Compliance

• Maintain quality assurance standards across all customer interactions

• Ensure compliance with company policies, data security, and regulatory requirements

• Create and maintain SOPs, workflows, and documentation

• Drive continuous improvement initiatives based on quality and performance metrics



Qualifications:

• Minimum 5+ years in operations, customer service, or workforce management

• At least 2 years in a supervisory or leadership capacity

• Background in healthcare, BPO, or service-based operations preferred

• Proven ability to lead teams, manage performance, and drive results

• Strong understanding of SLAs, KPIs, and operational performance metrics

• Experience with workforce planning, scheduling, and resource allocation

• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

• Data-driven mindset with the ability to analyze metrics and implement improvements

• Proficiency in CRM platforms (Salesforce, HubSpot), help desk tools, and reporting systems

• Strong problem-solving skills with a proactive, solutions-oriented approach

• Team-oriented leader who embodies MedPro’s values—Do the Right Thing, Figure It Out, and Win as a Team



Compensation & Benefits:

• $8-10/hr (based on experience), paid weekly.

• Permanent work-from-home

• 40 hours per week, aligned with U.S. business hours (CST, graveyard shift).

• Fixed weekends off (Saturday & Sunday)

• Work Anniversary Bonus

• No work with pay on US Holidays



Application Process

1. Submit your most updated resume to all of these
s:







2. Shortlisted candidates will complete a brief criteria skills assessment after the initial interview.



Equal Employment Opportunity Statement



MedPro Disposal is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristic.

title: TikTok Shop Account Manager (Affiliate Marketing Focus)
description:
Role Overview



We are a boutique growth firm looking for a TikTok Shop Account Manager with strong Affiliate Marketing experience to manage and scale up to 5 client accounts.



This role is part-time to start, with a clear path to full-time as you take on more client accounts and responsibility.



This is not a task-based role. You will be responsible for owning client outcomes, driving revenue through TikTok Shop, and leading strategic conversations with founders and executives.



You must be confident, proactive, and comfortable operating in a client-facing environment.



Key Responsibilities



-Manage up to 5 TikTok Shop client accounts end-to-end

-Build and execute affiliate marketing strategies to drive revenue growth

-Identify, recruit, and manage TikTok affiliates and creators

-Optimize product listings, campaigns, and creator partnerships

-Monitor performance metrics and make data-driven decisions

-Lead client communication, including updates, reporting, and strategy calls

-Collaborate with internal tea
mbers to ensure execution quality

-Continuously test and iterate on growth strategies



Client-Facing Expectations



-Confidently communicate with founders, CEOs, and C-level executives

-Lead strategic discussions (not just provide updates)

-Translate performance data into clear business insights

-Set expectations and maintain strong client relationships



Requirements



-Proven experience with TikTok Shop management

-Strong background in affiliate marketing / creator partnerships

-Excellent spoken and written English (clear, professional communication required)

-Ability to think strategically and not just execute tasks

-Comfortable being client-facing and leading calls independently

-Strong organizational and account management skills

-Ability to manage multiple accounts without quality dropping



Working Hours



-Part-time initially

-Must be willing to transition to EST working hours once managing full client load

-Opportunity to move into a full-time role based on performance and client capacity



Who This Is For



-You take ownership and think like a strategist, not just an executor

-You are confident speaking with high-level decision makers

-You want to grow with a high-performance, boutique team



Who This Is NOT For



-If you need constant direction or supervision

-If you are uncomfortable speaking with clients

-If you prefer task-based, non-strategic roles



Compensation



-Competitive weekly salary (based on experience)

-Opportunity for performance-based bonuses

-Long-term growth potential within the company



How to Apply



Please include:



-A short video introduction (1–2 minutes)

-Your experience with TikTok Shop and affiliate marketing

-Examples of results you’ve driven (metrics preferred)

-Your availability and current workload



We are building a lean, high-performance team. If you are strategic, accountable, and confident in client environments, we want to hear from you.
