title: Media Buyer / Paid Ads Specialist (Meta & Google Ads)
description:
Media Buyer / Paid Ads Specialist (Meta & Google Ads)



Compensation: $200/month (starting)

Growth: Rapid increase in pay as accounts and performance scale



?



About Us



Create By Influence (CBI) is a fast-growing marketing systems company helping businesses generate leads through structured, high-converting systems.



We combine CRM automation, paid ads, and conversion-focused funnels into a complete growth engine for our clients.



We are scaling quickly and looking for a reliable, execution-focused Media Buyer / Paid Ads Specialist to manage and optimize paid ad campaigns across multiple client accounts.



?



Role Overview



This is an execution + optimization role, not a high-level strategy role.



Your job is to:

• Launch campaigns using proven frameworks

• Monitor performance daily

• Optimize for leads and cost efficiency



You will NOT be responsible for:

• Sales calls

• Client communication

• Building funnels/websites

• High-level strategy decisions



?



Responsibilities



You will manage and execute:



Campaign Setup (Meta & Google Ads):

• Launch campaigns using provided structures

• Set up ad sets, targeting, and budgets

• Ensure proper naming conventions and organization



Ad Management & Optimization:

• Monitor campaigns daily

• Adjust budgets based on performance

• Kill underperforming ads

• Scale winning ads

• Test creatives and audiences (when instructed)



Lead Generation Focus:

• Optimize for cost per lead (CPL)

• Ensure campaigns are generating consistent inbound leads

• Flag issues quickly (high CPL, low conversions, etc.)



Tracking & Attribution:

• Ensure pixels and conversion tracking are working

• Verify leads are flowing into CRM (GoHighLevel)

• Troubleshoot basic tracking issues



Reporting:

• Provide simple weekly performance updates

• Track key metrics (spend, CPL, leads generated)

• Log changes made to campaigns



?



Workload & Structure



• Starting with multiple client ad accounts

• Clear campaign frameworks and SOPs provided

• Work is structured, repeatable, and system-driven

• Daily check-ins on performance expected



?



Compensation



• Starting Pay: $200/month

• Additional pay as more accounts are assigned

• Performance-based growth opportunities

• Long-term consistent work for the right person



This role is designed to grow as we scale.



?



Requirements



• Experience running Meta Ads 
/Instagram)

• Basic experience with Google Ads (preferred)

• Understanding of lead generation campaigns

• Ability to analyze basic metrics (CPL, CTR, CPC)

• Strong attention to detail

• Ability to follow systems and instructions exactly

• Reliable internet and communication



?



This Role is NOT For You If:



• You only want to focus on high-level strategy

• You dislike repetitive optimization work

• You are not responsive or consistent

• You need constant creative direction

• You overcomplicate simple systems



?



Preferred Candidates



• Philippines or similar time zone preferred

• Available for consistent weekly workload

• Experience working with agencies or multiple accounts is a plus



?



Growth Opportunity



As CBI grows:

• You will manage more ad accounts

• Your compensation will increase

• Potential to move into senior media buyer or team lead role



?



Application Instructions (IMPORTANT)



To apply, include the following:

 1. Your experience with Meta Ads and/or Google Ads

 2. Types of campaigns you’ve run (lead gen, ecom, etc.)

 3. Example results (CPL, ROAS, or leads generated)

 4. Tools you’ve used (Ads Manager, Google Ads, GHL, etc.)

 5. Your availability (hours per week)



?



Bonus (Optional – helps you stand out)



Pick a local business and briefly explain:

• What’s wrong with their ads (or what they’re missing)

• What you would improve in their campaign



?



We are looking for someone reliable, analytical, and ready to grow inside a fast-moving system.



If that’s you — apply now.

title: CRM & Client Intelligence Manager (Full-Time then Part-Time Retainer)
description:
The Stellaris Collection is hiring a CRM operator to build and maintain the central nervous system of our business. This role owns the accuracy, structure, and intelligence of our client database inside HubSpot CRM. Every client interaction must become clean, usable data that drives revenue.



We sell high-end luxury watches (Rolex, Patek Philippe, Audemars Piguet) and are expanding into a dedicated jewelry vertical. Transactions regularly range from $10,000 to $100,000+. Precision, discretion, and high-level logic are required.

 

Your Core Responsibility



Ensure zero clients are lost, miscategorized, or untracked. You will manage the full lifecycle of client data:

Capture ? Clean ? Enrich ? Organize ? Activate

 

What You Will Do



1) Data Capture Oversight (Daily)

• Monitor incoming contacts from Podium (SMS/Webchat), Outlook, and Shopify

• Ensure all contacts are properly synced into HubSpot; create, identify and fix broken automations in Zapier or Make

• Own CRM data flow integrity across all systems (Podium,
, Shopify)



2) CRM Data Management & Hygiene

• Merge duplicate contacts and standardize naming conventions (Proper Case, no shorthand)

• Maintain a complete, single source of truth for each client (no duplicate or fragmented records)



3) Client Tagging & Intelligence

• Categorize every lead by Brand Interest, Client Tier (Spend), Buyer Type (Collector/Dealer), and Budget Range

• Extract and log key client signals (important dates, specific watch requests, preferences, urgency)



4) Deal Tracking & Reporting

• Move deals through pipelines accurately (Sales vs. Sourcing)

• Create a deal whenever a client shows clear buying intent or discusses pricing

• Generate weekly/monthly lists: Top LTV clients, Inactive high-value leads, and jewelry upsell opportunities

 

Compensation & Long-Term Structure



This role is structured in two distinct phases to reward the heavy lifting required at the start:



Phase 1: The Build (First 2–3 Weeks)

• Workload: Full-time (40 hours/week) for historical audit and setup

• Rate: $8–$10/hour depending on experience (top candidates will be hired at the high end)

• Focus: Importing historical data, cleaning duplicates, and mapping custom properties



Phase 2: The Pulse (Long-Term Maintenance)

• Workload: Part-time (approx. 10 hours/week)

• Pay: $400–$500 monthly retainer (based on performance and speed)

• Focus: Daily data audits, tagging new leads, and preparing outreach lists

• Note: As our jewelry vertical scales, this role has the potential to grow back into a larger position

• This role is designed for long-term stability with consistent monthly income after the initial build phase

 

Requirements

• Expert-level HubSpot experience: You must understand custom properties, deal workflows, and pipeline logic

• Automation fluency: Experience with Zapier or Make is required

• Luxury mindset: You understand that data errors directly impact revenue and client experience at a high-ticket level

• Work hours: During Phase 1, a 4-hour overlap with PST is required (9am–1pm Los Angeles time)

 

Hiring Process (Strict Evaluation)



Step 1: Loom Video

Submit a 2–3 minute Loom video showing you navigating HubSpot. Show how you would:

• Create a contact

• Tag them for “Rolex interest”

• Move a deal stage



Step 2: Technical Test (Data Precision)

Top candidates will be sent 10 messy CSV rows to clean, standardize, and categorize.

100?curacy is the passing grade.



Step 3: Logic Test (Conflict Resolution)

Scenario: A Shopify order comes in for a Rolex, but the client is already tagged as a “Patek Collector.”

Explain how you would:

• Preserve historical data

• Expand tagging correctly

• Update the client profile



Step 4: Written Screening (Applied Thinking)

A new lead texts via Podium asking about a pre-owned Rolex Daytona. Budget is unknown.

Explain exactly:

• Which HubSpot properties you would populate

• How you would structure the record

• How you would prepare it for follow-up

 

How to Apply



Subject line MUST be:



The Stellaris Collection CRM Manager 2026



Include:

1. A brief summary of your HubSpot and automation experience

2. Your Loom video link

3. Your answer to the Step 4 written screening question

4. Strongly preferred: links to screenshots or a brief description of a CRM you’ve built or managed

title: Sales Rep
description:
Part-Time Cold Calling Sales Rep / Real Estate (Land)



3-Month Contract | 20 hrs/week | Opportunity to Extend



We’re not looking for average — we’re looking for someone who thrives on outperforming targets and turning discipline into results.



At our company, we operate by three core values: Relentless Excellence, Disciplined Results, and Continuous Innovation. These aren’t just words — they guide how we work every day.



This role is focused primarily on cold calling landowners, identifying selling opportunities, and qualifying motivated sellers to keep our acquisition pipeline strong. If you’re competitive, organized, and enjoy conversations that lead to deals, this role could be a strong fit.



This is a part-time contract role for 3 months with the opportunity to extend based on performance.



Please follow the application process



Do NOT call,
, or DM about this role



JOB DESCRIPTION Video: 



APPLICATION LINK: 



Company: 



Position Details:



- Pay: $10/hour + weekly performance bonuses

- Schedule: Monday – Thursday

- Hours: 2:00 PM – 7:00 PM (20 hours per week) EASTERN TIME ZONE

- Contract: 3-month contract with opportunity to extend

- Payment: Weekly (Mondays via 
)

- Start Date: ASAP



Role Responsibilities:



Cold Calling (Primary Focus)

- Call landowners to identify selling interest

- Build rapport and gather property details

- Qualify motivated sellers and identify potential deals



Lead Qualification

- Identify motivated sellers

- Segment prospects and move qualified opportunities forward



CRM & Reporting

- Log conversations and update records in the CRM

- Share notes and insights with the acquisitions team



Process Adherence

- Follow call workflows and company processes

- Provide feedback on messaging and lead quality



Collaboration

- Communicate with the team through internal chat

- Stay aligned with acquisition goals and pipeline targets



Attendance & Performance

- Hubstaff Monitoring: Tracks time, activity, and screenshots

- Punctuality is crucial: 3-strike rule. No-call/no-show = termination

- Communication: Active communication in GChat is expected



Role Requirements:

title: Executive Operations Assistant
description:
Executive Operations Assistant (Remote | Systems & Administrative Support)





We are hiring a detail-oriented Executive Operations Assistant to support a structured remote operations company.







This role exists to maintain internal systems, ensure execution consistency, and keep administrative workflows running without gaps.







If you are organized, reliable, and able to follow structured processes with precision, this is a long-term opportunity with stable work and consistent hours.



Role Overview





You will support internal operations by:



Managing documentation and SOP placement



Maintaining internal systems and trackers



Sending reminders and ensuring follow-through



Preparing reports and keeping data clean and usable







This is a systems-driven administrative role, with occasional light editing and file handling support.



What You’ll Be Responsible For





Core Responsibilities

Upload and organize SOPs in Notion and Google Drive



Maintain strict file naming and folder structure



Update internal records based on approved instructions



Send administrative reminders and track completion



Monitor missing logs and flag issues



Prepare weekly reports (Monday-ready)



Assist with onboarding administration (documents, quizzes, tracking)







Additional Support (Light Editing)

Make simple edits to documents, PDFs, or internal materials



Format files for clarity and consistency



Upload and organize media/files into the correct locations



Route finalized materials for approval when needed







Daily Work Includes

Task tracking and updates



System organization and cleanup



Following up on incomplete or delayed items



Sending end-of-shift summaries



What This Role Is NOT

Not a decision-making role



Not customer support or social media



Not a high-level creative or design role



Not a flexible “figure it out as you go” environment







This is a precision-based execution role with clear structure and expectations.



Requirements

Strong attention to detail



Ability to follow instructions exactly



Highly organized and structured



Reliable communication







Technical Comfort

Google Chrome



Google Drive



Notion (or similar tools)



Basic time tracking tools







Editing & File Handling

Comfortable making basic edits and formatting adjustments



Ability to keep documents clean, consistent, and organized



No advanced design skills required, but must be comfortable handling files and simple edits



Compensation & Hours

Trial Period (10–14 days): $2.50/hour



Post-Trial Compensation: $3.00–$5.00/hour based on accuracy, reliability, and execution quality







Hours & Work Structure

Trial: 15–20 hours per week



Post-trial: ~20 hours per week baseline



Opportunity for additional hours and consistent scheduling based on operational needs







Compensation Structure

Pay is evaluated based on:



Accuracy of work



Task completion without follow-up



System organization and consistency



Adjustments are made based on execution quality and workload responsibility







Position Stability

This is a long-term administrative role, not project-based work



Work is structured, repeatable, and consistent



Clear expectations, defined systems, and ongoing workload



Work Expectations

High accuracy and attention to detail



Clean task tracking and reporting



Strong deadline discipline



Consistent availability during scheduled hours



Clear, concise communication



Application Instructions (IMPORTANT)





To apply, include the following:



A short introduction about your experience with administrative or organizational work



Tools or systems you’ve used (Notion, spreadsheets, etc.)



Answer this question:



“How do you keep track of multiple tasks and deadlines?”



Include the word “STRUCTURE” at the top of your application

description:

title: Sales Representative
description:
Join a fast-growing US online home goods retailer and enjoy the learning and opportunities that you have while working with us.



We strive to provide 5-star service & shopping assistance to all of our clients, and all of our team is accountable to fulfill this.



We are looking for a Sales Representative who can assist prospective buyers and close online ecommerce sales.



We're only looking for full-time, long-term, ongoing cooperation.



Work schedules available:

8 AM to 4 PM US EST

4 PM to 12 AM US EST



Your work week will be 5 days per week, with 2 days off per week. Days off will be selected with your manager. We will try to accommodate your preferences for which days you like, based on availability.



This role starts ASAP!!



Job Duties and Responsibilities:

Receive incoming phone calls and live chats from potential customers (prospects) who indicated that they need shopping assistance. This does not include calls and chats from existing customers that need help with their orders (that’s a job for a different department)

Makes follow-up calls, writes follow-up
s, and sends text messages to potential buyers. We try to follow up 5x with each person that contacted us (unless they indicate that they are no longer interested). We don’t do cold calling. Only reaching out to prospects who interacted with our online store and left their contact information.

After each interaction with a prospect/customer, writes notes & comments in our CRM (Asana)

Communicate efficiently and build rapport with potential customers

Assists prospects in their shopping journey and helps make the purchase decision

Ensures that all prospects’ questions and objections are addressed

Leads every interaction to a close sale, which means an order is placed on our online store

Prepares custom quotes, price matches competitors if necessary, and offers custom discounts. This requires calculating margins & ensuring profitability.

Answering questions about the products we offer, including technical questions. Learns the specifications, features and benefits of our bestsellers. Over time, becomes an expert in the industry.

If not able to answer product-specific questions, conducts research in the materials provided by the supplier, or reaches out to the supplier to get the information

Confirms stock availability and lead times when offering products to prospects

Documents standard procedures and best practices in a written form, so that other tea
mbers can learn from them



Requirements:

At least 2 years of experience in sales on the phone

Experience must include both inbound and outbound sales

Excellent written and verbal English communication skills

Experience in Customer Service on the phone is a nice to have

Experience in the e-commerce (online shopping) industry is nice to have

Home improvement, construction, and gas plumbing industry experience is a plus

Outgoing, friendly, and lively

Can-do mindset, with a sense of ownership, pride in your performance, and its impact on the company’s success

Long-term commitment - treat this as your career



What we can offer:

Sales commission: 8% of gross profit from all orders closed. Gross profit means the sale amount minus the product and shipping costs. Paid monthly. No upper limit. Monthly commission payouts range between $1,200 (the guaranteed minimum) and as high as $3,000 (for the top performer). The average monthly payout is $1,465 (average from the last 2 years).

$1,200/month minimum guaranteed pay

15 days paid days off per year (also convertible to min. guaranteed commission)

Paid training on all aspects of the role

Opportunities for professional development

Friendly atmosphere

Online Interview process

Fully remote position

title: Roadside Assistance Inbound Sales & Dispatch Specialist
description:
(Growth Pathway to Sales Manager / Team Leader)



Company: Staff Connect (on behalf of our client)

Location: Remote – Philippines-based

Schedule: Rotating shifts between 5:00 PM – 9:00 AM AEDT

Employment Type: Full-time

Compensation: Competitive salary (based on experience and qualifications)

Leave: One week paid leave per year



About the Client



Our client is Australia’s largest misfuelling specialist, providing nationwide roadside assistance to motorists who a
 put the wrong fuel in their vehicle. When drivers are stressed and stranded, their team steps in to turn the situation around — quickly, professionally, and with empathy.



Staff Connect is supporting the client in building their first offshore team in the Philippines, offering a genuine opportunity for high performers to grow into leadership roles and eventually manage an entire offshore operation.



The Role

Inbound Sales & Dispatcher



Growth Pathway: Sales Manager / Team Leader



You will start in a frontline Inbound Sales & Dispatch role, handling incoming calls, assisting distressed customers, and coordinating technicians across Australia.



As you demonstrate strong sales ability, attention to detail, and initiative, you’ll progressively take on operations, quality control, and leadership responsibilities, with a clear pathway to advance into a Sales Manager / Team Leader position.



Key Responsibilities

Core Dispatch & Inbound Sales

- Act as the first point of contact for motorists who have misfuelled their vehicle.

- Use empathy and strong communication skills to convert inbound calls into confirmed bookings.

- Coordinate technicians nationwide using booking platforms and real-time tools.

- Collect deposits, log jobs accurately, and manage cases from start to completion.

- Communicate with customers and technicians via phone, Gmail, Slack, and SMS



Daily Operations & Quality Control

- Support fleet job closures, including managing long wait times when calling for PO numbers.

- Track spill kit usage by collecting all related
s and invoices in Xero (create only, do not send).

- Maintain accuracy in Xero and GeoOp, including:

- Reviewing previous day’s jobs for incomplete entries.

- Identifying jobs not properly closed or not sent to Xero.

- Perform call log clean-ups, ensuring:

- Objection reasons are correctly logged.

- ETAs, phone times, and job numbers are accurate.

- Sales opportunities are identified and closed.

- Discounts are properly logged and ticked.

- Act as a second set of eyes on GeoOp and call logs to catch errors, fill gaps, and prevent missed opportunities.



Documentation & Process Support

- Update fleet documents using new templates for consistency and clarity.

- Assist with process mapping, staff training, and continuous improvement initiatives.



Tech & Customer Support

- Coordinate servo runs and tech card runs to keep dispatch lines clear.

- Take ownership of escalated customer issues requiring extended phone support.

- Be comfortable being on camera during Slack huddles as part of daily operations.

- Participate in Slack huddles during shifts, including training, check-ins, and live support.



The Successful Candidate

- Minimum 2 years’ experience in a call centre, sales, or dispatch role.

- Proven success in sales conversion and customer service.

- Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.

- Calm under pressure, with strong empathy for customers in stressful situations.

- Tech-savvy, with proficiency in Google Chrome, Gmail, and Google Workspace.

- Comfortable using Slack, including joining huddles and appearing on camera when required.

- Experience working from home with reliable internet and a quiet workspace.

- Bonus: Experience in automotive, roadside assistance, or mobile repair.

- Bonus: Knowledge of Australian geography.



What’s in It for You

- Fully remote, work-from-home role.

- Work with a growing international client.

- Comprehensive training and ongoing support.

- Clear career progression: Dispatcher ? Sales Manager / Team Leader.

- Supportive, professional team culture.

- Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.

title: Executive assistant to a small business owner
description:
Title:

Executive Assistant (Healthcare & Business Operations) – Full Time, Long-Term



Job Description:



We are a US-based medical practice and small business looking for a highly organized, proactive Executive Assistant to support a physician-owner.



This is NOT a basic virtual assistant role.

You will act as a central coordinator to ensure that nothing is missed, delayed, or forgotten.



You will help manage day-to-day operations across:



Medical practice coordination

Billing follow-up

Calendar and scheduling

Business tasks and reminders



We are looking for someone reliable, detail-oriented, and strong in follow-up.



Responsibilities:



Manage and maintain a master calendar (appointments for the owner only not for the medical clinic, deadlines, events)

Track all important deadlines (taxes, insurance, utilities, renewals)

Follow up with billing company to ensure claims and statements are processed (no billing , just to make sure biller is doing the job on time)

Confirm that patient billing statements are printed and mailed on time

Maintain a master task list and follow up until tasks are completed

Coordinate communication between staff members and business partners

Track employee schedules, time off, and coverage (5-6 employees)

Maintain venue/event calendar and updates

Send daily and weekly summary reports to the owner



IMPORTANT:



This role requires someone who:



Follows up consistently

Does NOT forget tasks

Does NOT wait for instructions repeatedly

Can remind and follow through until completion



Requirements:



Excellent English communication (spoken and written)

Prior experience as an Executive Assistant (REQUIRED)

Strong organization and time management skills

Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines

Proactive mindset (you take initiative)

Comfortable making calls and following up

Experience with Google Sheets, calendar systems, and task tracking tools



Work Schedule:



Full-time (30-40 hours/week)

Must overlap with US daytime hours (Central Time preferred)



Compensation:



$5-7/ hour

Long-term stable position

Opportunity to grow with the business



How to Apply (IMPORTANT):



To apply, please answer the following:



Describe your experience as an Executive Assistant.

How do you track multiple deadlines and tasks?

If a billing company does not respond for 2 weeks, what would you do?

Please record a short voice message (1–2 minutes) introducing yourself.



Applications without answers will not be considered.

title: Wordpress Web Developer
description:
Role Overview

You will be responsible for building WordPress websites from approved Figma designs using Beaver Builder and our standard development setup. This includes creating clean, responsive page layouts, working with ACF and structured content, and configuring plugins to support each build.



You should be comfortable working within an established system and able to translate designs into well-organised, maintainable websites. From time to time, the role may involve light customisation using PHP or custom functions, as well as working with hosting environments such as cPanel or WHM.



The focus is on delivering consistent, high-quality builds that are reliable, easy to manage, and aligned with Yellow Design’s approach.



This is currently a part-time role with the potential to grow into a full-time position.



---

About Us

Yellow Design is a New Zealand-based web design studio focused on building clean, high-performing WordPress websites for small to medium businesses.



We use a consistent stack and value simple, maintainable builds over complex or over-engineered solutions.



We are looking for a reliable WordPress developer to help build websites from approved designs.



---

Key Responsibilities

- Build WordPress websites from approved Figma designs

- Create clean, responsive layouts using Beaver Builder and Beaver Themer

- Set up and configure plugins based on project requirements

- Implement structured content using ACF, custom fields, and custom post types

- Ensure websites work well across desktop, tablet, and mobile

- Perform basic troubleshooting and resolve layout or functionality issues

- Prepare websites for review and handover

- Follow established build processes and standards within Yellow Design

- Communicate clearly within tasks and flag any issues early



---



Skills & Experience

- Strong experience with WordPress website builds

- Confident using Beaver Builder (experience with Beaver Themer is highly preferred)

- Experience working with ACF, custom fields, and structured content

- Ability to build from Figma designs with attention to detail

- Solid understanding of responsive design

- Comfortable setting up and managing plugins

- Basic PHP knowledge and ability to write or modify simple functions

- Experience working with staging environments

- Familiarity with cPanel or WHM hosting environments

- Good communication and ability to work independently



---

Bonus Skills (Not Required, But Nice to Have)

- WooCommerce experience

- Stronger PHP or custom development capability

- Experience building reusable layouts or templates

- Performance optimisation experience

- Experience with SEO-friendly builds



---



What We Offer

- Flexible remote work. You manage your own hours

- Ongoing work with the chance for regular projects

- Clear systems and expectations so you know what to do

- A supportive working environment where your work is valued

- Direct communication and feedback

- Opportunity to grow with Yellow Design over time



---

How to Apply

If this sounds like a good fit, we’d love to hear from you! Please send an
 with:



- A brief introduction about yourself

- Examples of websites you've developed or worked on

- Any relevant skills or experience related to this role

- A note of your favourite Youtube Channel!

- Please start your
 with the word ‘WEB DEV’ so I know you read this job post carefully.






Subject: Website Developer Application

Video Editing

Technical Requirements

Job Details

How to Apply

To confirm you read this post carefully, start your application with the word “ONCALL.”



This role is best suited for someone who values long-term cooperation, clear expectations, and consistent quality, even with flexible hours.

title: Operations Coordinator + Guest Support
description:
OPERATIONS COORDINATOR + GUEST SUPPORT



MAC Property Ltd is a boutique short-term rental management company based in New Zealand. We manage approximately 50 properties and pride ourselves on delivering seamless guest experiences and efficient operations.



This role is a mix of set administration hours and incoming communication monitoring hours.



______________



THE ROLE



Overseeing the day-to-day operations across our portfolio, taking ownership of guest experience and ensuring smooth operational execution.



• Promptly respond to guest messages within 10 minutes

• Manage guest issues and take ownership through to resolution

• Communicate with and manage our team of cleaners, including sharing guest feedback

• Order supplies for our properties

• Download, filter, and review data using Excel, ensuring a high level of accuracy with no errors

• Recruitment and onboarding of new cleaners

• Submit damage claims for reimbursement

• Monitor reviews and implement improvements where needed

• Organise repairs and maintenance



• Administration hours: Extremely varied and constantly changing, ranging from recruitment to supply orders and bookkeeping tasks, while also juggling guest and cleaner communications as they come in. This role requires constant task-switching — moving between admin work and live communication without losing focus or accuracy.



• Message monitoring hours: Respond within 10 minutes. Focused purely on guest and cleaner communication, including handling issues as they arise (e.g. a missed clean or faulty dishwasher). You are not required to be deskbound during these hours, but must remain available and responsive. There are often long periods with little to no activity, balanced with times where you’ll need to step in and resolve an issue as it arises.



______________



ABOUT YOU



• At least 4 years’ experience in a professional, structured environment where accuracy, accountability, and responsibility are expected — this is not an entry-level role

• At least 3 years’ experience working with customers, including confidently resolving and de-escalating issues

• Friendly, confident, professional communicator — not timid or rattled by strong personalities or difficult conversations

• Naturally good with people — builds rapport and trust quickly, reads the room, and communicates in a warm, effective way that gets things done

• Confident making decisions and taking ownership of outcomes, without constant escalation

• Strong common sense and able to think on your feet when situations don’t go to plan

• Excellent administration and organisational skills, with a naturally structured approach

• Confident with Google Drive, Excel, Word

• Happy to pick up the phone via WhatsApp when needed

• Must supply your own MacBook (Windows will not work with our systems) and phone with strong, reliable data coverage



______________



WHY JOIN US?



• Supportive and professional team

• A role that’s varied, interesting, and people-focused

• Long-term, stable role with guaranteed fortnightly pay + 13th month included



______________



WEEKLY SCHEDULE



The schedule is based on New Zealand time and aligns well with morning hours in the Philippines — no overnight shifts required



• Monday: 10:00am – 3:00pm (Admin) + 3:00pm – 10:30pm (Message Monitoring)

• Tuesday: Off

• Wednesday: 10:00am – 1:30pm (Admin)

• Thursday: 10:00am – 1:30pm (Admin)

• Friday: 10:00am – 1:30pm (Admin)

• Saturday: Off

• Sunday: 10:00am – 10:30pm (Message Monitoring)



Important: Dec 15 to Jan 15 is our peak season, so no additional time off is permitted outside your normal days off. One 1–2 day break may be arranged with prior approval.



______________



PAY



• USD $5.50/hour

• Admin Hours: Full hourly rate

• Message Monitoring: Paid at 60% of hourly rate

• Remain available and respond within ~10 minutes

• Outside of this, no other work is required — you are not deskbound during these hours

• 27 paid hours per week

• Fortnightly pay: USD $297.00



______________



APPLICATIONS



We want to get to know YOU.



Please send us a personalised message via this platform (this will act as your cover letter — not written using AI), and include all of the below:

 1. Why this role appealed to you

 2. Details of your specific experience that match the requirements outlined in our ad

 3. A Google Drive link to your CV (ensure “anyone with the link” access is enabled)



Applications must be submitted via this platform only. Please do not contact us directly.



We look forward to hearing from you — many thanks.

title: Part-Time Shopify Store Builder & Launch Specialist for Kids Toy E-Commerce Brand
description:
We are looking for an experienced part-time Shopify Store Builder & Launch Specialist to help us create, design, and launch a professional online store for our kids toy e-commerce business.



This role is ideal for someone who knows how to build a clean, trustworthy, high-converting Shopify store from start to finish. Our brand sells kids toys, so the website should feel fun, bright, safe, and parent-friendly while still looking polished and professional.



You will be responsible for setting up the store, designing the pages, organizing products and collections, and making sure everything is ready for launch.



Responsibilities:



Build and set up a complete Shopify store from scratch

Choose and customize a Shopify theme to fit our brand

Create a clean, modern, and high-converting storefront

Design the homepage, product pages, collection pages, about page, contact page, and policy pages

Upload products, product descriptions, images, variants, and pricing

Organize collections and navigation menus

Set up apps needed for reviews,
 capture, upsells, bundles, or other conversion tools

Configure store settings, shipping, payment gateways, taxes, and basic policies

Optimize the store for mobile and desktop

Ensure the website loads well and looks professional

Help prepare the store for launch and test that everything is working properly

Follow branding direction and make revisions based on feedback



Requirements:



Proven experience building and launching Shopify stores

Strong understanding of Shopify themes, apps, and store setup

Good design taste with the ability to create a professional and trustworthy brand look

Experience with e-commerce conversion best practices

Experience organizing products and collections properly

Strong attention to detail

Good English communication

Ability to work efficiently in a part-time role and meet deadlines

Bonus if you have experience with kids, family, toy, or consumer product brands



Nice to Have:



Graphic design skills

Copywriting for product pages and homepage sections

Experience with Shopify SEO basics

Experience with Klaviyo or
 marketing setup

Experience creating branded banners and visuals



What We’re Looking For:

We want someone who can take initiative and build a store that is not just visually nice, but also ready to convert customers. You should understand what makes an e-commerce store trustworthy and easy to shop. Since this is a kids toy brand, the store should appeal to parents and gift buyers.



This is a part-time role with the potential for ongoing work if it’s a good fit.



To Apply:

Please send:



Links to Shopify stores you have built

A short message explaining your experience with Shopify

Your favorite Shopify store you have built and why

Your expected hourly rate or fixed project price

The word TOYLAUNCH in your application so we know you read everything



Screening Questions:



Have you launched a Shopify store from start to finish before?

What apps do you usually recommend for a new Shopify store?

How would you make a kids toy store look trustworthy and high-converting?

How long would it take you to build and launch the store?

Are you available for part-time work on an ongoing basis?

title: PART-TIME REAL ESTATE VA - GoHighLevel CRM Specialist
description:
IMPORTANT: To apply, begin your message with the words "GHL Ready". Applications without this will not be reviewed.



ABOUT US

We are a high-performing real estate team specializing in Manhattan, the Bronx, and Westchester. We focus on residential sellers and manage a large contact database through GoHighLevel CRM. We have documented SOPs and a structured training plan ready for you on Day 1.

s and texts after calls

HOW TO APPLY

Send ALL of the following. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

Your resume highlighting real estate VA experience

description:

About Us



We are an e-commerce incubator behind a portfolio of 7–8 figure direct-to-consumer brands across beauty, lifestyle, and health & wellness.



Our mission is to build products that make people feel something—confidence, comfort, and inspiration—and ensure our creative reflects that same emotional impact.

title: URGENT! Multimedia Artist (Graphic Design + Video Editing + Web/UI-UX) Project-Based ? Possible Full-Time
description:
Hey, you.

Yeah, you — the one who can make a brand look like a million bucks, cut a video that actually gives people chills, and probably has opinions about kerning and loading speeds that most people in your life don't want to hear about. We see you. We need you.

We're Redfern Media, a digital marketing agency out of Dallas, Texas that's been in this game for nearly two decades. We've worked with some names you'd recognize — folks from Shark Tank, Dr. Benjamin Hardy (bestselling author of Personality Isn't Permanent and Who Not How), Robin Sharma (yes, the guy who wrote The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari), and a growing roster of founders, visionaries, and companies doing genuinely cool things. We move fast, we care about craft, and we take the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously.

Right now, we're bringing on a Multimedia Artist for a project-based engagement — and if you're as good as we think you are, this has the very real potential to turn into something full-time.



Why We're Hiring Right Now

We've just onboarded a really exciting new client — a health-tech marketplace company preparing for a major investor pitch event in late April. Think: pitch decks, a two-minute brand video, one-pagers, booth materials. It's a tight timeline, the stakes are real, and we need someone who can hit the ground running without needing their hand held. After the initial sprint, there's a full website overhaul, ongoing brand creative, and more work lined up — so this isn't a one-and-done.



What You'll Be Working On First (The April Priority)



> Refine and produce a 2-minute brand/pitch video (scripted, edited, polished — the kind that makes people stop scrolling)

> Design 3–5 pitch slides to accompany a live investor presentation

> Refresh booth display materials for a live event

> Create a clean, compelling investor one-pager

> Polish up an investor pitch deck — visually, not just content-wise





What Comes After (The Bigger Picture)

Once we clear April, here's what the ongoing work looks like:



> Full website redesign — homepage overhaul, core pages, audience-specific landing pages (this is where your UI/UX chops come in)

> Ongoing brand creative — ads, social assets,
 visuals, collateral

> Sales and partner materials — guidebooks, onboarding decks, partner-facing one-pagers

> Video content as the brand scales — explainer videos, campaign content, event assets

> Whatever else comes up, because it always does





What We're Looking For

> 5–8+ years of hands-on experience as a multimedia artist, creative generalist, or similar

> Rock-solid graphic design skills — you know your way around Adobe Creative Suite, Canva Pro, whatever the job needs

> Strong video editing chops — you can take raw footage or a script and turn it into something people actually want to watch

> Web design and UI/UX experience — you don't just make things look good, you think about how people move through them

> You work well under a deadline without the drama

> You can take a creative brief and run with it, but you're also not afraid to ask a good question

> Strong eye for detail — we notice when something's off, and so do you

> Bonus if you have experience working on pitch decks, investor materials, or fast-moving startup environments





The Details

Type: Project-based to start, with strong potential to transition to full-time

Rate: $8–$10/hr

Timeline: We need someone who can start quickly — like, NOW





To Apply

Head to 
/careers and fill out the form. Show us some work. Tell us a little about yourself. We actually read these.



Draye Redfern

CEO / Founder - Redfern Media / Fractional CMO & Other businesses

title: Graphic Designer & Marketing Content Specialist
description: Storite is an Australian company specialising in warehouse storage solutions, including pallet racking, shelving, mezzanines and automation systems, working with major clients across Australia. We are seeking a Graphic Designer & Content Creator (part-time, remote) to support our marketing team by producing clean, professional content across digital and print platforms. The role will involve designing LinkedIn-focused social media posts, case studies, brochures, capability statements and contributing to the development of our 2025 product catalogue, as well as editing product images and creating simple graphics such as icons and layouts. We are looking for a designer with a strong eye for modern, minimal and well-structured design, with particular attention to spacing, alignment and typography, and the ability to maintain consistency across all materials in line with our brand. The ideal candidate will have proven graphic design experience with a strong portfolio, proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (InDesign, Illustrator and Photoshop), experience designing for both print and digital, and the ability to follow direction and feedback effectively, with good English communication skills. Experience with catalogue or brochure design, B2B or industrial brands, and basic video editing will be highly regarded. This is a flexible part-time role (approximately 15–25 hours per week) with some overlap with Australian working hours preferred, offering a competitive hourly rate based on experience. Applicants should provide a short video introduction, a portfolio of relevant work, and examples of previous design projects.

title: Amazon Product Research VA (SellerAmp & Keepa Required) – Part-Time

I am looking for an experienced Amazon Product Research Virtual Assistant who can find profitable and accurate product opportunities. This role is focused on quality over quantity, and I need someone who understands proper sourcing — not guessing or estimating.



Responsibilities:

Ensure all data is accurate and verifiable

Update the product list every day

Lift Video Production is a Melbourne-based video production company creating content across safety and training, recruitment and culture, and sales and marketing.



We work with frontline industries including transport and logistics, healthcare, infrastructure, and civil construction, producing content that’s used in real environments, from on-site inductions through to tenders, websites, and campaigns.



Alongside client work, we’re investing heavily in our own content. This includes social videos, on-site content, and original ideas that help grow the business and bring in new opportunities.



We’re looking for an experienced video editor to join us and play a key role in both our internal content engine and client delivery.



 The Role



This role will initially focus heavily on Lift’s own content, including:



Social videos (LinkedIn, Instagram)

On-site pieces and quick-turn content

Vlogs and director-led content

Testing new ideas and formats



As you settle in, you’ll also:



Support client projects across case studies, training, and brand videos

Take ownership of full edits where appropriate

Help shape stories from interviews and real-world footage

Potentially take on more projects as we grow



You’ll be working closely with the director, often taking rough ideas or footage and turning them into polished, engaging content.



What We’re Looking For



We’re after a mid–senior editor who:



Knows how to tell a story, not just cut clips

Is comfortable working with fast-moving, less structured content

Can take a rough idea and bring it to life

Works efficiently without sacrificing quality

Brings creative thinking and problem-solving to a brief

Takes ownership and pride in their work

Communicates clearly and confidently



This is not a junior role. You should be comfortable making decisions and elevating the final output.



Tools & Workflow



Required:



Adobe Creative Suite

Strong experience in Premiere Pro

Working knowledge of After Effects



Nice to have (but not required):



Motion graphics or text animation

Strong instincts for social content

Sound design or advanced audio cleanup



Workflow tools we use:



Google Workspace (Drive, Docs, etc.)


 for reviews and feedback

Asana for project management



You’ll often be given a brief or even just raw footage and expected to craft something strong from it.



 Location & Availability



We’re based in Melbourne, Australia

Remote position

Ideally, you can work with some overlap to Melbourne time

Clear deadlines and expectations per project



Trial & Growth



We’ll do a paid trial project

The goal is a long-term working relationship

Opportunity to grow with us as our content output scales



Culture & Team



Lift is practical, hands-on, and focused on doing work that actually matters.



A lot of what we create is filmed in real environments, warehouses, trucks, job sites, so the content isn’t polished-for-the-sake-of-it. It needs to feel real, clear, and engaging.



You’ll fit in well if you:



Enjoy working on real-world content

Like moving fast and testing ideas

Care about doing high-quality work

Want to contribute, not just execute



Important Application Note



To show you’ve read this job description carefully, include the phrase:



“two hands on the wheel”



Any application that does not include this phrase will not be considered.

title: Part-Time Web Graphic Designer – Visual Styling & Branding | U.S. Agency | Figma
description:
We are a U.S.-based digital marketing agency building websites for home service businesses — HVAC, plumbing, roofing, and general contractors. We are looking for a part-time graphic designer with a strong visual eye to join our small, focused team.



This is not a development role. You will not be building websites. You will be applying visual design — color, typography, imagery, and branding — on top of pre-built wireframe structures. Our team handles all WordPress development.



How our design process works:

*************************

We use structured wireframe component libraries (Bricks Builder for WordPress) that define page layout and section structure. Your job is to take those grayscale wireframes and transform them into visually polished, on-brand designs in Figma. The structure is already solved — you bring it to life visually.



Think of it as: we hand you the blueprint, you make it beautiful.



What you'll be doing:

*************************

- Applying brand colors, typography, and imagery to pre-structured wireframe layouts in Figma

- Selecting and directing imagery that fits the brand and industry

- Creating consistent visual systems across multiple pages of a website

- Producing social media graphics using established brand assets (between website projects)

- Delivering clean, organized Figma files ready for developer handoff



What we're looking for:

*************************

- Strong instincts for color, typography, and visual hierarchy — this is the most important thing

- At least 1–2 years of graphic or web design experience

- Proficiency in Figma

- An eye for what looks premium vs. generic — we build websites that stand out

- Good written English and reliable communication

- Consistent availability during U.S. Mountain Time business hours (some daily overlap required)

- Stable internet connection



What we are NOT looking for:

*************************

- WordPress developers or coders

- Designers who rely heavily on templates or AI-generated layouts

- Generalists who do "a little bit of everything"



Portfolio requirement — non-negotiable:

*************************

Do not apply without a portfolio. We are specifically looking for evidence of strong visual design judgment, not just technical ability. Show us:



- 3–5 web design projects demonstrating brand application, color use, and typography choices

- Behance or Dribbble profile strongly preferred

- If no online portfolio exists, send direct links to 3 projects with a brief explanation of your design decisions on each



Work that looks like stock templates or generic website builders will not be considered.



Hours: Part-time, 20 hours per week to start (approximately 4 hours per day). Potential to grow toward full-time as project volume increases.



Salary: $175–$225/month USD based on portfolio quality and experience. Paid weekly via PayPal or Payoneer.



To apply:

*************************

Do not message through OnlineJobs.ph. Complete our short application form here: 



You will be asked to:

*************************

1. Share your portfolio link

2.  Describe your design process in 3–5 sentences — specifically how you approach color and typography choices

3.  Find one website in the home services industry (HVAC, plumbing, roofing, etc.) that you think is well-designed and explain specifically why

4.  Confirm your hours availability and time zone



Shortlisted candidates will be given a paid test task — applying visual styling to a provided wireframe using a simple brand guide. This is how we make our final decision.



Applications without a completed form will not be reviewed.

title: Marketing and CRM Coordinator
description:
Storite is an Australian company specialising in warehouse storage solutions, including pallet racking, shelving, mezzanines and automation systems, working with major clients across Australia. We are seeking a Marketing & CRM Coordinator (full-time, remote) to support our sales and marketing operations through effective management of our CRM system,
 campaigns, website updates and general marketing coordination. The role will involve maintaining and updating our CRM (Odoo), ensuring data accuracy and supporting the sales team with lead tracking and pipeline management, as well as building and executing
 marketing campaigns, uploading and updating website content (WordPress), and assisting with the coordination and scheduling of marketing activities including social media. The ideal candidate will be highly organised, detail-oriented and proactive, with strong written and verbal English communication skills, and experience working with CRM systems,
 marketing platforms and website content management. The ability to follow structured processes, manage multiple tasks and work independently is essential, while experience with Odoo, SEO content updates and B2B marketing environments will be highly regarded. This is a full-time role (40 hours per week) with some overlap with Australian working hours preferred, offering a competitive salary based on experience, and an opportunity to grow within a fast-paced and supportive business environment.

title: Lead Generation & Data Specialist (B2B Industrial / Equipment)
description:
Job Title: Lead Generation & Data Specialist (B2B Industrial / Equipment)



Location: Remote (Philippines Preferred)



Type: Full-Time (40 hrs/week)



---



About Us



We are National Equipment Supply, a rapidly growing industrial equipment supplier specializing in frac tanks and related equipment across industries like oil & gas, construction, environmental services, and agriculture.



Our mission is simple:

Become the go-to supplier for companies that need reliable, high-quality equipment — fast.



We are building a high-performance outbound sales engine and are looking for a detail-oriented Lead Generation Specialist to help us identify and build high-quality prospect lists.



---



Your Role



Your primary responsibility is to find, verify, and organize high-quality business contacts that our sales team will reach out to.



This is NOT a basic scraping job.



We are looking for someone who:



1. Understands how to find the right people at the right companies

2. Ensures data is accurate and verified

3. Takes pride in delivering clean, usable, high-quality lead lists



---



What You’ll Be Doing



Research and identify companies in target industries:



  * Oil & Gas

  * Environmental Services

  * Waste Management

  * Construction

  * Refineries

  * Agriculture & Water Treatment



Find and verify key decision-makers:



  * Operations Managers

  * Procurement Managers

  * Project Managers

  * Equipment / Fleet Managers

  * Directors of Operations

  * Business Owners



Use tools such as:



  * Apollo

  * Clay

  * Outscraper

  * LinkedIn

  * Company websites

  * Google search



Verify
s using tools like:



  * MillionVerifier (or similar)



Build clean, structured lead lists inside:



  * Google Sheets / Airtable / CRM



---



Data You Must Capture



For every contact, we require:



* First Name

* Last Name

* Company Name

* Job Title

*
 Address (VERIFIED — very important)

* Phone Number (if available)

* Industry

* Location

* Company Size (if available)

* Website

* Source (where you found the contact)



Bonus (not required but valuable):



* Notes on company (projects, activity, relevance)

* LinkedIn profile



---



What We Care About Most



1. Accuracy > Volume

We prefer 100 high-quality leads over 1,000 low-quality ones.



2. Relevance

The contact MUST be someone involved in equipment decisions.



3. Verification

We will not use unverified
s.



4. Organization

Clean, structured data that is easy for our team to use.



---



Ideal Candidate



* Has experience in B2B lead generation

* Understands how to find decision-makers (not just random contacts)

* Is highly detail-oriented and organized

* Takes ownership and pride in their work

* Is comfortable using multiple research tools

* Has strong written English (for data accuracy)



---



Performance Expectations



* Daily / weekly lead targets (to be set together)

* Low bounce rate on
s (high accuracy)

* Consistent delivery of clean, usable data



---



### ???? Compensation



* Competitive hourly or monthly rate (based on experience)

* Performance bonuses based on lead quality and output

* Opportunity for long-term role as we scale



---



How to Apply



To apply, please include:



1. A brief introduction about yourself

2. Your experience with lead generation tools (Apollo, Clay, etc.)

3. Examples of lead lists you’ve built (if available)

4. The word “NES” in your application (to show attention to detail)



---



Final Note



We are building something big — and this role is a key part of that growth.



If you are someone who takes pride in delivering high-quality work and wants to be part of a fast-growing company, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Operations Supervisor
description:
Operations Supervisor (Remote)





Shift Execution & Team Performance Oversight**







We are hiring an Operations Supervisor to oversee real-time execution across a remote team.







This role is responsible for attendance compliance, performance enforcement, and day-to-day operational control. You will serve as the first point of accountability during active shifts, ensuring tea
mbers meet standards and that all issues are addressed, documented, and resolved.







This is a structured management role focused on execution and discipline, not a creative or flexible position.



Position Summary





The Operations Supervisor is responsible for maintaining consistent execution across assigned operators. This includes enforcing attendance standards, monitoring performance, and ensuring all operational issues are handled with urgency and precision.







Success in this role is defined by:



High attendance consistency



Low error rates



Complete and accurate documentation



Minimal unresolved issues during shifts



Key Responsibilities





Attendance & Workforce Compliance

Monitor and enforce daily attendance across assigned operators



Identify and act on no-shows in real time



Ensure all attendance issues are properly documented



Maintain consistent reliability standards



Performance Oversight

Monitor operator performance and quality trends



Issue behavior and coaching logs when standards are not met



Follow through on all performance issues until resolved



Identify recurring issues and escalate when necessary



Shift Execution & Issue Management

Act as the first point of contact during active shifts



Manage operator questions related to scheduling and execution



Ensure all issues are:



Addressed promptly



Documented accurately



Resolved or escalated as needed



Operational Documentation & Tracking

Maintain:



Attendance and reliability logs



Performance and coaching logs



Shift issue tracking



Ensure all documentation is complete, accurate, and submitted on time



Requirements

Strong attention to detail



Clear and direct communication



Ability to manage multiple people and issues at once



Highly organized and consistent



Ability to follow and enforce structured processes



Technical Requirements

Google Chrome



Google Drive



Experience with trackers, logs, or structured systems



Comfortable working in a remote, process-driven environment



Compensation & Schedule

Hourly Rate: $2.00 – $5.00 USD



Hours: 20–30 hours per week







This role begins in a ramp-up phase, where you will be trained on systems, expectations, and daily workflows while gradually taking on responsibility.



Phase 1: Trial / Ramp-Up (2–3 Weeks)

Hourly within the lower end of the range



Focus on learning systems, shadowing, and handling initial responsibilities



Evaluation based on reliability, organization, communication, and follow-through



Phase 2: Expanded Responsibility

Increased ownership of attendance, logs, and operator management



Movement within the hourly range based on performance



Eligibility for performance-based bonuses, including:



Attendance consistency



QA and performance discipline



Accuracy and completion of logs



Phase 3: Full Role Transition (Performance-Based)

Opportunity to transition into a base + performance compensation structure



Includes:



Fixed base compensation



Performance bonuses tied to team outcomes (attendance, QA, execution)







This transition is based on proven consistency, ownership, and ability to manage operations independently.



Performance Expectations

Maintain high attendance across assigned operators



Ensure performance issues are identified and resolved promptly



Keep all logs accurate, complete, and on time



Minimize unresolved issues during shifts



Application Instructions (IMPORTANT)





To apply, include the following:



A brief summary of your experience managing or supervising others



A specific example of how you handled a tea
mber who was not meeting expectations



Answer this question:



What steps do you take to ensure issues are fully resolved, not just reported?



Include the word ACCOUNTABILITY at the top of your application







Applications that do not follow instructions will not be considered.



Selection Process

Application review



Interview



Trial period



If you are comfortable managing people, enforcing structure, and ensuring consistent performance, apply now.

title: Executive Assistant - Remote
description:
Company Overview



Join the leader transforming healthcare waste management across America.



MedPro Disposal is the nation's largest privately-held medical waste management company, serving 35,000+ providers across 48 states in a $2+ billion market. Since 2009, we've been the trusted partner helping healthcare facilities save up to 30% on waste management costs while maintaining 99% on-time service.



What we do: We lead with comprehensive medical waste disposal solutions, plus pharmaceutical waste management, secure document destruction, and compliance training services—all powered by innovative technology and a client-first approach.



Why it matters: We handle the critical but complex challenge of medical waste management so healthcare providers can focus on what they do best: delivering exceptional patient care.



Our impact: From physician practices to hospitals, dental offices to long-term care facilities, we're trusted advisors and strategic partners—not just vendors. We combine proven expertise with environmental responsibility and comprehensive protection to solve complex operational challenges.



Ready to grow with us?

Join a fast-scaling, mission-driven company where we Do The Right Thing, roll up our sleeves with a "figure it out" attitude, and win as a team. We're building something meaningful—making healthcare safer, more compliant, and more efficient—one client at a time.



Position Overview

MedPro Disposal is seeking a highly proactive and experienced Executive Assistant to support executive leadership across multiple business entities. This role sits within our Executive Division under Executive Operations, directly supporting senior leadership in driving day-to-day effectiveness and execution.



This is a high-impact, remote role requiring someone who can operate independently, manage competing priorities, and communicate effectively in an asynchronous environment. The ideal candidate brings 7–10+ years of experience, strong organizational discipline, and the ability to engage confidently with executives—both verbally and in writing.



Beyond task execution, we are looking for an impact-driven professional who can demonstrate how they’ve made leadership roles easier and more efficient—improving workflows, streamlining communication, and supporting company priorities and direction.



The ideal candidate is also highly tech-savvy, with experience leveraging AI tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT) and platforms like Asana or similar project management tools to enhance productivity, organization, and overall operational efficiency.



Key Responsibilities



Executive Support & Impact

• Manage and optimize executive calendars, priorities, and scheduling across multiple stakeholders

• Proactively identify ways to save time, reduce friction, and improve executive productivity

• Track deliverables, follow-ups, and key priorities to ensure consistent execution

• Demonstrate ownership in making leadership workflows more efficient and scalable



Communication & Coordination

• Draft, edit, and manage executive-level communications 
s, updates, reports, presentations)

• Serve as a liaison across teams, ensuring clear, timely, and effective communication

• Support executives in both synchronous and asynchronous environments

• Ensure alignment across stakeholders, priorities, and deadlines



Operations & Organization

• Prepare and organize reports, dashboards, and executive-level materials

• Maintain structured systems for tracking tasks, projects, and key initiatives

• Manage competing priorities with a high level of organization and attention to detail

• Anticipate needs, identify gaps, and proactively solve problems



Technology, AI & Tools

• Leverage AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude) to streamline workflows, documentation, and communication

• Utilize Asana (or similar tools) for task tracking, project management, and accountability

• Continuously identify and implement tools or systems that improve efficiency and collaboration



Confidentiality & Trust

• Handle sensitive information with a high level of discretion and professionalism



Required Qualifications

• 7–10+ years of Executive Assistant experience supporting senior leaders

• Proven ability to communicate effectively with executives (written and verbal)

• Demonstrated track record of creating impact—improving processes, saving time, and supporting leadership effectiveness

• Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities

• Experience working remotely in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment

• Tech-savvy with proficiency in Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and collaboration tools



Preferred Qualifications

• Experience supporting executives across multiple companies or business units

• Familiarity with Asana or similar project management tools

• Experience using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to improve productivity

• Background in reporting, operations support, or content creation

• Experience working with U.S.-based teams or clients



Key Traits for Success

• Highly proactive and impact-oriented

• Strong communicator with the ability to engage effectively with executives

• Organized, detail-oriented, and execution-focused

• Able to anticipate needs and operate with minimal direction

• Committed to supporting leadership and company goals



Compensation & Benefits:

• $5-8/hr (based on experience), paid weekly.

• Permanent work-from-home

• 40 hours per week, aligned with U.S. business hours (CST, graveyard shift).

• Fixed weekends off (Saturday & Sunday)

• Work Anniversary Bonus

• No work with pay on US Holidays



Application Process

1. Submit your most updated resume to all of these
s:







2. Shortlisted candidates will complete a brief criteria skills assessment after the initial interview.



Equal Employment Opportunity Statement



MedPro Disposal is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristic.

title: Meta Ads Buyer & Google Ads Specialist
description:

 Manage

 Funnel Optimization (Ongoing)

 Scaling (As Performance Allows)

 Identify campaigns ready to scale based on consistency and profitability

 Implement gradual budget increases while maintaining ROAS (our framework: +20% every 3 days if stable)

 Manage risk during scaling—know when to scale aggressively vs conservatively



 Reporting & Communication (2x Weekly)

 Create Loom video updates (Tuesdays + Fridays) covering: what's working, what's not, why, and next steps

 Maintain real-time performance dashboards

 Present strategic recommendations in weekly team strategy calls

 Translate data into insights that non-marketers can understand



 What You Bring



 Must-Haves (Non-Negotiable)



 Experience:

 2+ years hands-on experience managing
/Instagram ad campaigns (ideally for education, coaching, or info products)

 Proven track record scaling campaigns to $3K+/day profitably

 Deep understanding of VSL funnel mechanics (ad ? video ? application ? high-ticket sale)

 Experience managing $50K+/month ad budgets with clear ROAS accountability



 Technical Skills:


 Ads Manager mastery (campaign structure, pixel optimization, audience building)

 Basic funnel-building tools (ClickFunnels, Leadpages, or similar)

 Data analysis in Google Sheets/Excel (pivot tables, cohort analysis, dashboards)

title: URGENT! Full-Stack Web Developer (Webflow + WordPress + GoHighLevel)
description:
A little about us first.

I'm Draye Redfern, and I run Redfern Media — a digital marketing agency based out of Dallas, Texas that's been doing this for nearly two decades. We've had the privilege of working with some incredible people — names from Shark Tank, bestselling authors like Dr. Benjamin Hardy (Personality Isn't Permanent, Who Not How) and Robin Sharma (The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari), and a whole lot of founders building things that actually matter. We're a lean, fast-moving team that takes the work seriously — and we're growing.

Right now, we need a web developer who can come in, get up to speed quickly, and help us build something great for a client that's gearing up for a major market launch.



What's the Project?

We're working with a health-tech marketplace company — think precision nutrition meets automation meets a platform that genuinely hasn't existed before. They're preparing for a full go-to-market launch, and the website needs to be ready for it. That means clean, fast, conversion-focused, and built to serve multiple audiences at once — consumers, partners, producers, investors, and more.

This is a 3-month project-based engagement to start. If things go well — and we expect they will — there's a real path to something longer term.



What You'll Be Building

> A fully overhauled Webflow website — homepage, core pages, and audience-specific sections built for clarity and conversion

> Audience-specific landing pages — for consumer onboarding, partner recruitment, producer applications, and investor outreach

> Clean user flows and journey architecture — from first visit to the right next step, with as little friction as possible

> Strong CTA structure and conversion logic baked into every key page

> Mobile-first, fast-loading builds — no bloated code, no sluggish experiences

> Tracking and analytics integration — GA4, pixel setup, and the foundational infrastructure needed to actually measure what's working

> SEO and AEO foundation — proper structure, metadata, and technical SEO built in from the start, not bolted on after (a plus, not required)



A Note on Tech Stack

This project is primarily Webflow — so that needs to be your strong suit. But we want to be upfront: most of our other client work lives in WordPress (Elementor and Divi), and our funnel and landing page work runs through GoHighLevel. You don't need to be an expert in all three on day one, but familiarity matters. As this engagement grows — and as you potentially grow with us — you'll likely be touching all of them.



What We're Looking For



> 5–8+ years of web development experience, with Webflow as a genuine strength — not just something you've dabbled in

> Solid understanding of conversion-focused design and UX principles — you build pages that do something, not just look pretty

> Comfortable working across WordPress (Elementor & Divi) and ideally familiar with GoHighLevel

> You understand how a website fits into a broader marketing and sales funnel — not just how to build pages

> You can take a creative brief or a rough wireframe and move forward without needing constant check-ins

> Detail-oriented — broken links, inconsistent spacing, and slow load times bother you on a personal level

> You communicate clearly and can flag blockers early rather than going quiet





The Details



> Type: Project-based (3 months) with strong potential to transition to full-time

> Rate: $8–$10/hr

> Start: We're moving on this quickly, so ideally you're available soon





To Apply

Go to 
/careers and fill out the form. Include links to Webflow projects you've built — live sites preferred. We look at everything that comes in.





Draye Redfern

CEO / Founder of Redfern Media & Fractional CMO

title: Web Developer
description:
PROJECT TITLE:

Landing Page & Lead Capture Funnel for Industrial Equipment Concept (With GIF/Video Integration)



OVERVIEW:

We are developing a new industrial product/tool and need a simple but high-quality landing page to validate market interest before full development.



This is NOT a typical website project.



The goal is to create a conversion-focused landing page that presents product features visually and collects structured feedback from potential customers.



PROJECT OBJECTIVE:

Build a single-page (or very simple multi-page) website that:

- Showcases short GIF/video clips of product features

- Allows users to click into features for more detail

- Collects user feedback and interest (lead capture)

- Is optimized for clarity, simplicity, and conversion



Think of a simplified version of a Kickstarter product page—but focused on industrial buyers.



TARGET AUDIENCE:

- CNC machine shops

- Aerospace and aviation companies

- Automotive performance shops

- Manufacturing and fabrication businesses

- The design should feel professional, industrial, and clean (not playful or consumer-focused).



SCOPE OF WORK:

1. Landing Page Design

Clean, modern layout

Mobile-friendly (responsive)

Fast loading (important for GIF/video)

Clear structure:

Headline + value proposition

Feature sections (with GIF/video)

Call-to-action (feedback / interest form)



2. Feature Display Section

Grid or scroll-based layout showing:

Short looping GIFs or video clips

Short captions (1–2 lines)

Ability to click each feature to:

Expand

Or open a deeper section/page with more detail



3. Feedback / Lead Capture Form

Simple form that collects:

Name

Company

Industry


Level of interest (dropdown or buttons)

Open-ended feedback

Optional (bonus):

Conditional questions based on answers



4.
 Capture + Basic Integration

Store responses in:

Google Sheets OR

Airtable OR
