title: Accounts Payable (AP) Specialist
description:
The Company



Del Playa Group (DPG) is a U.S.-based professional services firm specializing in accounting, finance, and audit solutions for businesses and CPA firms. We help clients expand capacity, strengthen controls, and elevate performance by offering three pillars: fractional CFO advisory, top offshore talent, and transformative technology.



The Role



We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Accounts Payable (AP) Specialist to support our U.S. operations. This role will be responsible for managing end-to-end accounts payable processes, purchase order workflows, expense reporting, and vendor compliance.



The ideal candidate is proactive, systems-savvy, and capable of managing high-volume transactions with accuracy and efficiency. You will play a key role in maintaining strong financial controls, ensuring timely payments, and supporting overall accounting operations.



Key Responsibilities:



Purchase Orders



•Review purchase order (PO) requests for completeness and accuracy

•Convert approved PO requests into purchase orders and invoices

•Ensure proper documentation and alignment with company policies



Accounts Payable

•Set up ACH batches in Intacct

•Process ACH payments through the bank

•Manage and monitor the shared “Accounts”
 inbox

•Process vendor invoices accurately and timely in Intacct

•Post recurring bills and ensure proper coding

•Handle credit card transaction processing and reconciliation

•Perform W-9 verification via IRS website

•Prepare and post AP-related journal entries

•Support annual 1099 preparation and reporting



Expense Reporting (Brex)

•Set up and maintain Brex user accounts

•Review and validate Brex credit card charges

•Conduct weekly expense reimbursement reviews



Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

2–4+ years of accounts payable or general accounting experience

Experience with ERP systems (Intacct preferred) and expense tools (e.g., Brex)

Strong understanding of AP processes, vendor management, and payment cycles

Familiarity with U.S. compliance requirements (W-9, 1099 reporting)

Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Microsoft Office/365

Ability to work U.S. hours (PST) with flexibility as needed



Key Attributes for Success



Strong attention to detail and accuracy in high-volume processing

Excellent organizational and time management skills

Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

Strong communication skills, both written and verbal

High level of integrity and professionalism

Collaborative mindset with the ability to work cross-functionally

Proactive problem-solving and process improvement mindset



Perks and Benefits

Professional Development: Ongoing training and exposure to U.S. accounting practices

Career Growth: Clear progression path within the accounting organization

Global Exposure: Work closely with U.S.-based teams and clients

Work Flexibility: Remote work environment with collaborative tools

title: CapCut Video Editor
description:
** Please Read Entire Post Before Contacting **



Hello, currently in need of a video editor that has experience with CapCut and ElevenLabs (very easy to use). I have 20 faceless YouTube channels. I am looking to hire 2 assistants to split the workload or one assistant available that can handle the 20.



I will provide access to all paid accounts. The channels are audio based, visuals are not important. Do not source separate clips based on the content of the videos except for rare occasions. We will recycle the same 5-6 backgrounds and rotate them daily across all videos. Every video each day will have the same background across all channels, we simply change it each day.



You can simply save the long background clip separately once made in CapCut and keep it in a folder for future use as it cuts down editing time significantly.



The process would be as follows:



- I would provide the script, you paste the script and generate the audio in ElevenLabs, download the audio, then create a basic video in CapCut with subtitles for the audio.



- The backgrounds can be looped clips from Pexel or Envato, stretched to match the time of the audio (usually 10-20 minutes). 



- You download the video and upload to YouTube. I would provide the video titles, descriptions, tags, category, and the ChatGPT prompt to generate the thumbnail.



- You simply need to edit the videos so that it flows well and to generate the subtitles.



Must be knowledgeable in editing videos and must be able to know how to use CapCut. ElevenLabs is easy to use and I will provide a tutorial video going over the process to confirm the job.



Will be working about 3-5 hours a day as the videos only take about 10 minutes each to edit, I can show how in the tutorial videos.

title: Cinematic Video Editor - Events, Corporate & Storytelling
description:
About Us



We are a U.S.-based video production company focused on creating high-end cinematic videos for corporate clients, events, and brand storytelling. We create story-driven videos that combine interviews, music, and cinematic b-roll to deliver emotional and engaging content. While our focus is cinematic storytelling, occasional social media-style projects may be included based on client needs. We also do a great deal of photography work, mostly events and headshot style photography. 



IMPORTANT – Please Read Before Applying



We are NOT looking for: TikTok editors, YouTube vlog editors or fast, high-volume editing



We ARE looking for: A cinematic editor who understands storytelling, pacing, and emotion



Role Overview



You will be responsible for editing: Event highlight videos, cinematic intro videos, corporate storytelling videos, multi-cam full-length speaker event videos, templated projects and occasional photography editing projects. 



This is a growth opportunity. The position will start as a part time position, but there's opportunity to grow into a full-time role. 



These projects require:

Strong sense of story

Clean, professional editing

Ability to cut to music and build emotion



What You’ll Be Doing:

Edit cinematic videos (1–3 minutes), including event highlights and brand storytelling

Edit multi-cam speaker presentations (30–40 minutes)

Assist with templated projects and occasional photo editing

Combine interviews and b-roll into a cohesive story

Select and cut music appropriately

Maintain a clean, modern, professional editing style

Work closely with the creative director to bring high-quality storytelling projects to life.

Deliver first drafts for internal review



Requirements:

Strong understanding of cinematic storytelling

Ability to edit based on emotion, not just timing

Experience with event videos, weddings, or documentaries

Ability to use interviews to drive narrative

Strong attention to detail and ability to take feedback and improve quickly

Clean editing style (no overuse of effects)

Good English communication

Reliable and meets deadlines



MUST HAVE (Portfolio Requirements)

You must provide 2–3 examples of:

Cinematic videos

Event highlights OR wedding films OR brand storytelling



Applications without relevant portfolio will NOT be considered



Bonus Skills (Not Required)

Sound design

Color grading experience

Motion graphics editing

Photo editing - Lightroom

Experience with corporate clients



Paid Test Project



We will provide a short paid test project to selected candidates.



This helps us evaluate:



Your storytelling ability

Your pacing and editing decisions



Compensation

Starting at $8–$12/hour (based on experience)

This position starts as part-time (10–20 hours/week), with strong potential to grow into a full-time role

Consistent project flow for the right candidate

Performance-based pay increases over time

Bonus opportunities available after long-term engagement



How to Apply (IMPORTANT)



To apply, include:



Portfolio links (REQUIRED)

A short message answering this:



What makes a video feel “cinematic” to you?



Your favorite type of project to edit



Applications that do NOT follow instructions will be ignored

Include the word “Cinematic” at the top of your application so we know you read this.

title: SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflo

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Positioning & Launch Specialist
description:
Type of Work: Part-Time

Hours Per Week: 10

Compensation: $300/month to start

Location: Philippines



Job Overview



We are looking for a high-level marketer based in the Philippines to help us with the positioning, messaging, and overall launch strategy for a new product called the RoofFirst Kit, while also helping us align it with our roofing lead-generation business, Hampton Roofing Experts.



This is not a virtual assistant role.

This is not an account setup role.

This is not a general admin support role.



We already have support for account setup and execution tasks. What we need now is someone with real marketing experience who can help us think through the market, shape the offer, improve the messaging, and guide the launch process.



We are looking for someone who understands how to help bring a product to market the right way.



What You Will Help With



1. Product Positioning



Help us clearly position the RoofFirst Kit in the market



Refine the value proposition and core offer



Identify the strongest customer pain points, buying triggers, and product angles



Help differentiate the product from competitors and low-quality alternatives



Help us determine how to best connect the product to our service business





2. Launch Strategy



Help build the overall launch strategy from pre-launch through rollout



Advise on what should happen first, what should wait, and what is most important



Help prioritize the best launch channels and tactics



Help us identify what assets, messaging, and promotions are needed before launch



Help us avoid weak positioning and poor launch decisions





3. Messaging and Offer Development



Help refine the core marketing message for the product



Improve hooks, calls to action, landing page messaging, ad angles, and customer-facing copy



Help strengthen how we present the offer online



Assist with promotional ideas and conversion-focused messaging





4. Funnel and Conversion Strategy



Help us think through the customer journey from first impression to purchase or lead



Advise on how the websites, ads, and follow-up systems should work together



Help improve the conversion flow and launch readiness



Recommend what should be emphasized to build trust and increase action





5. Market and Competitive Insight



Help research competitors, similar products, and market opportunities



Help identify gaps in the market and positioning opportunities



Help evaluate where this product should be sold and how it should be introduced





Ideal Candidate



We are looking for someone who has real experience in some or most of the following:



product marketing



launch strategy



offer creation



direct response marketing



lead generation strategy



funnel strategy



conversion-focused messaging



e-commerce or product launches





You should be able to think strategically, not just complete tasks.



You should understand:



how to position a product



how to shape an offer



how to guide a launch



how marketing channels work together



how to improve messaging for conversion





We Are Not Looking For



a virtual assistant



a data entry person



a general admin support worker



a basic social media scheduler



someone whose main strength is setting up accounts or following checklists



someone whose experience is mostly clerical or assistant-based





Important Note



We already have support for setup and execution. We are specifically looking for someone who can bring marketing judgment, positioning insight, launch thinking, and strategic support to this project.



To Apply



Please answer all of the following:



1. What products, services, or brands have you helped launch?





2. What was your role in those launches?





3. Have you helped with product positioning, offer strategy, or launch planning before? Please explain.





4. What experience do you have improving messaging, conversion flow, or go-to-market strategy?





5. Share one example where your marketing input directly improved a launch, offer, or campaign.





6. Start your application with the words: RoofFirst Strategy







Main Goal



To help us position, present, and launch the RoofFirst Kit the right way so it has the strongest chance of success in the market.



If you want, I can also give you a shorter, punchier version that may perform even better on OnlineJobs.

title: Customer Service Representative for E-Commerce Account
description:

Join us from home to support top brands. Provide solutions, deliver exceptional service, contribute to client success, and grow your career with ongoing learning and advancement opportunities.

title: Webflow Designer for Service-Based Websites
description:
Are you a Webflow designer who leans more editorial, refined, and minimal than techy and heavy?



We’re looking for a designer to support ongoing client projects.

Our work is strategy-led, clean, and conversion-focused, with a strong emphasis on white space, hierarchy, and restraint.



This is not a role for “just build it” developers.

We care about how things feel, not just how they function.



THE TYPE OF WORK WE DO



We design websites for service-based businesses.



Think

Clean layouts

Strong hierarchy

Generous white space

Refined typography

Subtle interactions

Elevated, considered design



Not

Dark, heavy, tech-style builds

Over-designed layouts

Cluttered sections or excessive effects



If your portfolio leans corporate, SaaS, or developer-heavy, this likely isn’t the right fit.



WHY YOU’LL ENJOY WORKING WITH US



Clear direction. Strategy, copy, and wireframes are provided

Defined scope. No guesswork, no vague briefs

Flexible hours. Work to deadlines, not fixed shifts

Respectful collaboration. No last-minute chaos

Long-term partnership. Consistent work for the right person



We prioritise quality over speed.



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING



Build websites in Webflow based on provided strategy and wireframes

Translate brand direction into clean, high-end layouts

Structure CMS collections logically

Apply SEO basics during build

Refine and improve existing Webflow sites

Ensure all builds are easy for clients to manage



Optional



Design in Figma before build



REQUIRED SKILLS



WEBFLOW

Strong experience building in Webflow

Confident with CMS, components, and interactions

Clean builds only. No messy overrides or shortcuts



CLIENT-FIRST

Solid understanding of Client-First structure

Clear class naming and scalable systems



SEO BASICS

Proper heading structure

Meta titles and descriptions

Alt text and CMS SEO fields

Internal linking



THE NON-NEGOTIABLE



Your work must demonstrate

Strong use of white space

Clean, minimal layouts

High-end, service-based design

Thoughtful typography



We will not consider portfolios that



Feel overly masculine, tech-heavy, or corporate

Are cluttered or visually noisy

Prioritise effects over clarity



YOUR WORKING STYLE



You work well independently with clear direction

You communicate early if something’s off

You meet deadlines

You’re open to feedback

You take pride in clean, considered work



HOURS AND RATE



Part-time, ongoing

Flexible hours

Work to deadlines, not fixed shifts

Rate based on experience



HOW TO APPLY



2 to 4 live Webflow sites you personally built

At least one clean, service-based website

An example using Client-First

A short note on how you approach SEO in Webflow

Your hourly rate and weekly availability



Applications without relevant examples, do not reside in the Philippines or who do not provide the above will not be reviewed.

title: Credit Repair Specialist
description:
Part-Time Credit Repair Specialist & Mentor



I’m looking for an experienced Credit Repair Specialist to work with me part-time, helping not only with drafting and managing dispute letters but also teaching me the ins and outs of successful credit repair. I’d like someone who can guide me through the entire process — from creating effective dispute strategies and letters to freezing accounts, staying compliant, and keeping up with current credit repair methods.



This role is as much about mentorship as it is about doing the work. I already have access to Dispute Fox and some sample clients to work on, so you’ll have tools and examples ready to go. My goal is to dedicate about 2–4 hours a week to this process, learning hands-on while building real results together.



The schedule is flexible, the salary is negotiable, and I have no issue with you working elsewhere during our time together. If you’re knowledgeable in credit repair, confident in your dispute drafting skills, and enjoy teaching others how to succeed in this field, I’d love to hear from you.

title: Telesales Agent
description:
Telesales Agent – FMCG (Grocery & Liquor)



About the Role

We are looking for a driven and commercially minded Telesales Agent to support our field sales team by engaging retailers across Australia. This role is focused on calling independent grocery and liquor retailers, driving stock orders, new product ranging, and repeat purchases across our portfolio of brands.



You will play a key role in helping our brands win in-store, working closely with our field team to build strong retailer relationships and drive consistent sales growth.

Strong communication and relationship-building skills

 or CRM systems

title: Advice Paraplanner (Urgent)
description:
ADVICE STRATEGIST/PARAPLANNER

Positions Available: 2  |  Full-Time 

Location: Remote

Hourly Rate: AUD $10 – AUD $15 per hour

Monthly Salary: AUD $1,600 – AUD $2,400 per month 



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are a highly skilled, Australian financial advice firm seeking two experienced, hard-working, and responsible self-starters to support our financial advice clients and enhance our service through:

• Implementing financial advice (Statements of Advice / SOAs)

• Assisting with client enquiries and onboarding

• Personal insurance research and strategic analysis

• Preparing advice documents, research reports, and file notes

• Managing compliance workflows and maintaining accurate client records



ABOUT PURPOSE ADVISORY

We’re a heart-led, integrative professional services firm focused on helping Australians to live their most fulfilled life. We have a team of expert professionals based in Australia and a family-like back-end team based primarily in the Philippines. We’re all about personal growth and contribution, in every aspect - persona, relational, health and financial. As part of our team, you’ll contribute to Purpose Advisory’s mission to make a meaningful difference in our client’s and partners lives.



WHAT YOU WILL DO



SOA & Advice Preparation 

• Draft Statements of Advice (SOAs) and Records of Advice (ROAs) using adviser instructions and client data

• Prepare fact-find summaries and data collection packs for new and existing clients

• Compile product comparison research (super, insurance, investments) using approved research tools



Insurance Research 

• Run personal insurance quotes and comparisons across major providers

• Prepare insurance needs analysis and replacement summaries

• Track underwriting progress and follow up on outstanding requirements



Client Onboarding 

• Prepare client authority forms, engagement letters, and onboarding packs

• Coordinate platform account openings and rollovers

• Chase outstanding documents from clients and third parties



Compliance & File Management 

• Maintain compliant client files and ensure all advice documents are correctly stored

• Prepare pre-advice and post-advice checklists for adviser review

• Assist with audit preparation and regulatory lodgement tasks

Client Communication • Respond to client enquiries with approved language and escalate where appropriate

• Prepare portfolio review packs and meeting agenda summaries

• Draft follow-up
s after adviser–client meetings



AI-Assisted Research 

• Use AI tools to synthesise product research, generate comparison tables, and draft report sections

• Leverage AI for data extraction, scenario modelling, and document summarisation

• Identify opportunities to streamline workflows using AI prompts and automation



CANDIDATE MUST HAVE

• Australian financial planning experience

• Strong compliance knowledge to support product research, data management, onboarding, and implementation tasks

• Highly skilled with using AI to complete research, synthesise information, generate reports, and solve complex problems

• Experience using Google Workspace (Gmail, Google Drive, Docs, and Sheets)

• Especially strong skills with formulas and analysis in Google Sheets / Excel

• A stable and secure internet connection suitable for full-time remote work

• A reliable computer capable of supporting daily use of cloud-based systems and collaboration tools

• Clear written communication skills and comfort working with structured templates and prompts

• A proactive, organised approach and the ability to manage competing priorities



KEY TRAITS

• Methodical and detail-oriented

• Self-managed with strong follow-through

• Clear communicator

• Collaborative and adaptable across tasks and personalities



WE OFFER

• Training and support from a passionate, purpose-driven team

• Opportunities for long-term growth and stability

• A remote, flexible work environment where your contribution is truly valued



APPLICATION PROCESS

To apply, please submit the following:

• A 2–5 minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you are the best fit for this role

• Your updated CV and LinkedIn profile link

• Names and contact details of three references (2 professional + 1 personal)

• Your answers to the case study below



  Case Study

A client
s the financial adviser requesting urgent updates on their specific holdings in a certain stock which they are concerned about and are considering selling 50% of, which we manage for them. You know the adviser is going to be held up in meetings all day and will not even see the
. You also heard the adviser mention the day before that we plan to reduce our exposure to this one specific stock in our portfolios, due to recent market fluctuations and new technology risks that have arisen.

Q1: How would you respond to the client? What would you communicate to them, and what would you do behind the scenes?

Q2: If the client asked you to action an urgent sell-down of this stock before the end of the day, how will you react?



Please apply and
 all details to:

title: Social Media Artist – Short-Form Video, Motion, and Visual Storytelling
description:
Hi! I'm Seth. I'm a U.S.-based leadership coach and leadership researcher building a body of work at the intersection of leadership, meaning, courage, and what it means to live well.



If you want background on me before applying:





I am trying to build my personal presence online via vertical video. I will record all core videos myself. I am looking for a visual artist who can turn raw footage into short-form content that evokes awe, not just attention.



Claude Monet is my favorite painter, and Yosemite is my favorite place on earth. Why? It's because both evoke wonder through light and scale. That sensibility matters here, even if the medium is radically different (video instead of paint or granite cliffs). 



What you will do

 • Edit short-form videos (Reels / TikTok / Shorts) from weekly source recordings

 • Add captions, pacing, b-roll, and light motion graphics before cross-posting

 • Create clean, repeatable visual styles (typography, titles, lower thirds)

 • Cut and package highlights from my podcast appearances

 • Track and communicate performance metrics (views, retention, saves, shares), note patterns, and offer constructive feedback.



What I provide

 • Weekly (stream-of-consciousness and/or scripted) source recordings

 • Some direction and fast feedback as you get started (with a goal that feedback is barely needed at all since you would be the vertical video artist) 

 • Optional AI voice assets if ever needed 

 • A simple, respectful revision process



HOURS: Flexible. Start at 10–25 hours per week, with potential to grow.



?



HOW TO APPLY (READ CAREFULLY – APPLICATIONS ARE SCREENED FOR INSTRUCTION ACCURACY)



Applications that fail to meet any of the requirements below will not be reviewed.



0. Title



Put "Hi, I'm [Name], and I'm your next Social Media Artist."



1. Portfolio (Links Only)



Provide at least 7 short-form videos you personally edited.

Links must be viewable without requesting access.

No attachments. No uncurated folders.



2. Full CV or Resume



Include a complete CV or resume with:

 • work history,

 • dates,

 • tools used,

 • notable clients or long-term contracts.



3. References (Exactly Three)



Provide three professional references, each with:

 • full name,

 • relationship to you,

 •
 address or WhatsApp number.



4. Written Reflection (Six Paragraphs)



Write three (3) whole paragraphs answering:



What is awe-inspiring to you in the audio-visual arts?



You may reference painters, photographers, filmmakers, editors, or specific works. This is about taste, perception, and articulation. 



Write three (3) whole paragraphs answering:



What does the marriage of your artistic vision and technical prowess look like in action? 



5. Generative AI Disclosure



Include a section titled "Generative AI Use" stating:

 • whether you used AI in this application,

 • approximately how much (none / light / moderate / heavy),

 • what it was used for.



AI is allowed, though not preferred, in the part about which is wondrous to you (because AI is not you). Honesty is required.



6. Other stuff



Are you online anywhere I can find you? What else about you is interesting? What else might be relevant for this job? (Answer what you want from these and offer more if you'd like.)



?



Shortlisted applicants will be invited to a paid test edit.



If this post feels demanding, that is intentional.



_____



Since I'm asking you to be honest about AI, I used ChatGPT to generate this post, revised it a few times with additional prompts, and made final edits before posting. I used Grammarly to make my language clearer/tighter (since I tend to be verbose). I also used the onlinejobs.ph suggested salary tool.

 address

Work Schedule (Required)

title: Graphic Designer (Brochure Designer)
description:
PLEASE READ: INCLUDE YOUR UPDATED RESUME & BROCHURE PORTFOLIO in your application. IDEAL CANDIDATES MUST HAVE CREATED BROCHURES FOR COMMERCIAL SPACES AND/OR OFFICES.



Competencies and Qualifications



Must-Have



- Proven experience designing brochures, catalogs, or marketing materials for furniture, commercial products, or B2B environments

- Strong portfolio showcasing brochure designs with product-focused layouts (e.g., tables, fixtures, or similar items) across different use cases such as libraries, office spaces, and food courts

- Proficiency in Adobe InDesign (required); Illustrator and Photoshop experience preferred

- Ability to create clean, structured layouts that highlight product features, specifications, and real-world applications

- Solid understanding of print production processes (bleed, trim, margins, CMYK color setup, and prepress requirements)

- Strong knowledge of typography, grid systems, and visual hierarchy for multi-page brochures

- Experience preparing print-ready files and digital versions for client presentations or online distribution

- Proficiency with PC platforms and Adobe Creative Suite, primarily InDesign

- Familiarity with AI tools such as generative design tools, ChatGPT, and Adobe Firefly for concept ideation and workflow efficiency

- Comfortable completing a paid design test as part of the evaluation process



Key Qualities



Action-Oriented – Efficient in turning product information into polished brochure layouts with minimal supervision

Detail-Oriented – Ensures accuracy in product specs, spacing, alignment, and visual consistency across pages

Collaborative – Works closely with marketing, sales, and product teams to translate technical details into compelling visuals



What You’ll Work On



- Design brochures showcasing table collections across different environments (libraries, corporate parks, food courts)

- Create layouts that balance aesthetic appeal with functionality, highlighting durability, materials, and use cases

- Develop both print and digital-ready brochures for client presentations and marketing use



What We Offer



100% Remote Work – Work from anywhere in the Philippines

Career Growth Opportunities – Gain hands-on experience and continuous learning

Collaborative & Innovative Team – Work alongside skilled professionals who value excellence

title: Appointment Setter
description:
Our lead generation system is strong - delivering 100+ warm leads per month - but we need someone with a track record to pick up the phone and get these prospects on the calendar.



This role is for you if:



- You have a verifiable history of hitting or exceeding targets.

- You know how to handle objections, qualify prospects, and secure commitment over the phone.



The Opportunity:



Set Your Schedule: Part-time or full-time options available (Between 20 - 40 hours per week).



Work Aussie Hours: Shifts scheduled between 8 AM and 6 PM (Melbourne Time).



Uncapped Commissions: The more appointments you book that show up, the more you earn!



Must-Haves:

- Excellent spoken English with a confident, professional phone voice.

- Proven track record in outbound appointment setting, B2B sales, customer service or solving customer queries.

- Ability to provide references or past performance data.



If you are a closer who can pick up the phone and deliver results, you can start anytime with us!



Apply now by filling out the short form below -




/jobs/17033.64053.59877.1



Once you apply, we will send you more information.



IMPORTANT NOTE.



We will ONLY consider candidates who submit their application to our candidate management system through the link above.



About Us



At Performia, we help businesses through our flagship product called the Performia Journey, to transition their culture from being a place where people go to 'do a job' to a productive, safe and fun space where much like a sports team, everyone is motivated by accomplishment, and working towards the same goals.



It’s unlike anything else in the market and we need someone who thrives on creating opportunities.



We give businesses a complete system to create a high performing, safe and fun team culture, and it starts with knowing how to hire the right people.



Clients who build their team the Performia way, see that work stops feeling like work, and becomes a game the team is motivated to play.



Performia is a Swedish company established in 2002 with offices in 27 countries, launching in Australia in 2006.

title: Ecommerce Customer Support Specialist
description:
Customer Support Specialist - Ecommerce

— Remote | Full-Time | Philippines Only

— $850 - $1,100 USD/month

— 6AM - 2PM PHT / 6PM - 2AM PHT



At most eCommerce brands, support agents are treated like ticket machines.



You're handed a pile of canned responses and told to blast through the inbox as fast as possible.



Apologising for problems you flagged to the team months ago.



Nobody cares if the customer actually felt helped. They care that you hit your numbers.



Here, it's different.



Yes, we move fast. But never at the expense of making a customer feel genuinely heard.



You'll have the tools to work efficiently - but more importantly, you'll have the trust to use your own judgment.



Your thinking matters here. Your voice matters here.



So if you're someone who:

— Takes pride in writing
s that make customers say "wow, that was actually helpful"

— Wants to work for a brand that does something meaningful

— Thrives in fast-paced startups where you can grow



This role was built for you.





About Us



We're a wellness brand helping parents navigate their children's health.



These aren't casual purchases. When a parent reaches out, they're often worried, confused, or frustrated.



A lazy response can shatter their trust. But a thoughtful, empathetic reply? That turns a one-time buyer into a lifelong advocate.



Most eCommerce companies treat support as a cost to minimize. We treat it as the heartbeat of our brand.





The Role: What You'll Own



You'll manage our inbox with speed, empathy, and precision - reporting directly to our Customer Support Team Lead.



1. Inbox Mastery

Handle high-volume inbound
s with clarity and care. Every
 you send represents our brand.



2. Problem Solving

Don't relay information. Investigate, find the real answer, then deliver it in a way that puts the customer at ease.



3. Tone Advocacy

Maintain a warm, parent-to-parent tone that matches our brand voice. Know the difference between "technically correct" and "actually helpful."



4. Feedback Loop

You're our eyes and ears. When the same issue keeps surfacing, flag it and help us fix the root cause.





What We're Looking For



We're not looking for the fastest copy-paster. We need someone who can think, write, and genuinely care - all at the same time.



Must-Have Experience:

— 2+ years in Customer Support 
). eCommerce startup experience strongly preferred.

— Impeccable grammar and the ability to explain complex things simply.

— Familiar with helpdesk software (Gorgias, Zendesk) and eCommerce platforms (Shopify).

— A master of keyboard shortcuts and macros but knows exactly when to ditch the template and write something personal.

Must-Have Attributes:

— Empathy First - Calm and kind even when the inbox is on fire. You don't just respond. You make people feel heard.

— Startup Speed - Process changes don't throw you off. They energize you.

— Self-Starter - You see a gap, you fill it. You see a problem, you flag it. No hand-holding needed.

— Detail-Obsessed - A missing tracking link, a slightly off tone, a typo in a parent's name. You catch what others miss.





Compensation & Perks



Total Potential Compensation: $850 – $1,100 USD/month

— Base Salary: $850 USD/month

— Performance Bonus: Up to $250 USD/month, based on KPI performance and team contribution



Growth Path:

— Customer Support Team Lead - Mentor agents, oversee operations, shape support strategy.

— Social Media / Community Management - Transition into public-facing brand engagement.



We're a startup. When we grow, you grow. And we're growing fast.



Perks:

— 100% Remote - Work from wherever you're most productive

— 20 Days Paid Time Off per year

— HMO Coverage after 3-month probationary period

— Professional Development - Workshops, training, and certifications

— Small Team = Big Impact - Your work is visible, your voice matters, your ideas get heard





Our Hiring Process

No surprises. Here's exactly how it works:

1. Submit Your Application (linked below)

2. Loom Video Introduction

3. Technical Assessment

4. Interview with Customer Support Team Lead



We're building the #1 customer experience in the wellness space and we need someone who treats every
 like it matters.

If you're ready to do work that makes a difference, with a team that cares as much as you do - hit apply.




/

title: Capture & Business Development Lead (B2B – High-Value Contracts)
description:
Overview

We are building a high-performance unit focused on capturing and closing high-value B2B contracts in regulated and compliance-heavy environments.

This role is not transactional. You are responsible for identifying, shaping, and closing deals that directly drive revenue.

The priority is simple: bring in business.

______________________________________

Core Responsibility

You own the full lifecycle of deal acquisition:

From identifying opportunities ? positioning ? closing contracts ? feeding delivery teams.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

1. Pipeline & Opportunity Creation

• Identify and pursue high-value contract opportunities (RFPs, tenders, enterprise deals)

• Build and maintain a qualified pipeline (minimum 3–5x target coverage)

• Focus only on high-probability, high-value opportunities

________________________________________

2. Capture Strategy & Pre-Positioning

• Engage prospects before formal RFP release

• Build relationships with decision-makers

• Develop clear win strategies for each opportunity

________________________________________

3. Qualification & Targeting Discipline

• Rigorously evaluate opportunities based on:

-Deal size

-Win probability

-Strategic value

• Disqualify low-value or low-probability deals early

________________________________________

4. Internal Alignment & Execution

• Translate opportunities into clear capture strategies

• Direct internal teams (proposal, documentation, compliance) toward deal execution

• Ensure all efforts are aligned toward winning the contract

________________________________________

5. Deal Advancement & Closing

• Navigate complex, multi-stakeholder buying processes

• Drive deals forward with urgency and structure

• Close net-new business consistently

________________________________________

6. Revenue Ownership & Forecasting

• Maintain accurate pipeline and deal tracking

• Forecast revenue based on real deal progression

• Be accountable for contracts closed, not activity performed

________________________________________

Performance Expectations (Non-Negotiable)

• Build initial pipeline within 30–45 days

• Begin generating qualified opportunities immediately

• Target first closed contract within 60–90 days

• Maintain consistent deal flow and closing 
ntum

________________________________________

Required Qualifications

• Proven experience in B2B business development, capture, or enterprise sales

• Demonstrated ability to close high-value deals (?500K+)

• Experience in regulated or compliance-heavy environments

• Strong strategic thinking and structured execution

• High ownership and ability to operate independently

________________________________________

Preferred Background

• RFP / tender environments

• Government, infrastructure, or compliance-driven sectors

• Consulting, GovCon, or technical services

________________________________________

Compensation Structure

• Base Salary: ?50,000 – ?90,000

• Commission:

o 5?se per contract

• On-Target Earnings: ?90,000 – ?180,000+

• Additional incentives for:

o Multi-year contracts

o Multi-role placements

o Exceeding revenue targets

________________________________________

Success Profile

• Highly strategic and disciplined

• Focused on outcomes, not activity

• Strong communicator with executive presence

• Comfortable operating in long sales cycles and complex deals

• Relentless about bringing in revenue

________________________________________

Important Note

This role works alongside the contract acquisition team handling freelance/volume opportunities.

Your focus is different:

You are responsible for larger, more complex deals that drive significant revenue growth.

________________________________________

Bottom line:

If you are not closing deals, you are not succeeding in this role.

title: E-commerce Operations Manager (DTC Brand – Team Leadership & Systems)
description:
IMPORTANT: Only apply if you have real experience managing people and operations for a DTC e-commerce brand (physical products). If you have never led a team or owned outcomes — do NOT apply.



We are looking for a high-level E-commerce Operations Manager who will take full ownership of our day-to-day operations and team performance.



This is a leadership role. You will be responsible for managing people, building systems, and ensuring everything in the business runs smoothly.



The founder will focus on growth and creative strategy — you will ensure execution happens at a high level.



What you will be responsible for:



* Managing and leading the team (product listing, social media, operations)

* Holding tea
mbers accountable to performance and deadlines

* Running team calls and improving communication and structure

* Building and improving SOPs, workflows, and systems

* Overseeing Shopify store operations and execution quality

* Identifying problems early and solving them before they escalate

* Hiring, onboarding, and managing new tea
mbers

* Coordinating with suppliers and ensuring operations run efficiently

* Reporting directly to the founder with clear updates, insights, and solutions



This role is for someone who:



* Has real leadership experience in e-commerce (Worked as a Manager in a 6/7 figure business)

* Takes ownership and does not need hand-holding

* Thinks in systems, structure, and efficiency

* Is proactive and solves problems fast

* Can manage people and improve performance

* Communicates clearly and directly



Requirements:



* Proven experience as a manager with DTC e-commerce brands (physical products) (At least 6/7 figure businesses)

* Experience managing and leading a team

* Strong understanding of ALL Shopify operations

* Experience building SOPs and operational systems

* Ability to manage multiple moving parts at once

* Strong English communication (written and spoken)



We are NOT looking for:



* Basic Virtual assistants

* Beginners

* People who only execute tasks

* People without leadership experience

* People with only supply chain experience



TO APPLY:



Start your application with: **OPERATOR**



Answer all questions clearly and directly. Low-effort answers will be ignored.



1. Which DTC e-commerce brands have you worked with? (name, website, what they sell, approx. monthly revenue, your role)



2. What were you directly responsible for? (decisions + outcomes, not tasks)



3. How many people have you managed? (roles + how you managed them)



4. Describe a real operational problem you solved (what happened, what you did, result)



5. What systems or SOPs have you built? (short example)



6. What is your experience with Shopify operations? (what exactly have you managed)



7. What is your experience with suppliers / fulfillment?



8. If you join and see chaos in team + operations, what are the first 3 things you fix in the first 7 days?



* Include a Loom video (max 2–3 minutes) explaining your experience. (REQUIRED) 



Applications without the keyword or answers will be ignored.

title: Customer Experience Representative
description:
Job Title: Customer Experience Representative

Rate: $3 up to $5/hour  (based on experience)

Schedule: Must be available to work during the US (PST) time zone. 30 to 40 hours per week



We are seeking a Customer Experience Representative to join our dynamic team! This role is ideal for someone who excels at communication, learns quickly, and thrives in a fast-paced, customer-focused environment.

title: PR & Events Team: AI Growth Strategist
description:
Most PR teams send 20 pitches and hope for 1 reply.

You’ll send 200—and make each one feel personal.

At Penji, we’re not doing PR the old way.

We’re building AI-powered systems that outperform entire teams.

If you know how to scale outreach, content, and visibility using AI—this role is for you.



Your Mission

Use AI to scale PR outreach and content distribution—without sacrificing quality, personalization, or authenticity.



What You’ll Be Doing

AI-Powered PR Outreach (at Scale)

• Build systems to find, research, and pitch journalists, podcasts, and media contacts 

• Create highly personalized pitches at volume (no generic blasts) 

• Own the full pipeline: list building ? outreach ? follow-ups ? replies 

• Rapidly test hooks, subject lines, and angles 

• Turn every successful placement into a repeatable system 



Content Creation & Repurposing (10x Output)

o Clips, reels, quote graphics 

o Blog posts,
s, social content

• Adapt content for LinkedIn, YouTube, TikTok, Instagram 



Research & Trend Intelligence

• Use AI to spot trending topics and PR opportunities early 

• Research any target (journalist, brand, podcast) in minutes

Build Systems That Scale

• Track performance: placements, engagement, conversions 



Who You Are

• Strong writer—your pitches get opened and replied to

Why This Role is Different

title: Design Quality Control & Order Processing Assistant (Shopify)
description:
We are a U.S.-based growing ecommerce company specializing in custom metal business signs.



We are looking for a highly reliable, detail-oriented, and communicative assistant to manage Shopify order processing, file organization, and mockup coordination.



This role requires consistency, strong attention to detail, and the ability to follow structured systems daily. Fast and clear communication during working hours is very important for this position.



This is a long-term role with stable weekly pay, and the opportunity to grow into a larger role as the business expands.



SCHEDULE:

• 4 hours per day (guaranteed)

• Monday – Friday

• Split shift preferred:

  - 2 hours morning

  - 2 hours evening

• Occasional weekends during busy seasons (holidays)



WORK EXPECTATIONS:



• Morning order deadline: 11:00 PM Philippines time  

  (must be completed by 10:00 AM U.S. Central time)



• Evening designer-prep deadline: 10:00 AM Philippines time  

  (must be completed by 9:00 PM U.S. Central time)



• These deadlines are critical to ensure orders are processed on time for production each day.



• Consistent availability, responsiveness, and clear communication during scheduled working hours is required.



TASKS:

• Check Shopify orders daily

• Download customer logos from orders

• Create and organize folders in Google Drive

• Upload and manage files using a structured system

• Send design mockups to customers via email

• Track and log revision requests

• Move files between folders based on order status

• Prepare design queue for our designer

• Move approved designs to production folders

• Order backlight components from Amazon (using provided links only)

• Update a simple order tracking sheet



TOOLS YOU WILL USE:

• Shopify

• Google Drive

• Gmail

• Amazon

• Google Sheets

• ShipStation (basic use)



REQUIREMENTS:

• Strong attention to detail (very important)

• Fast and consistent communication during working hours

• Ability to follow step-by-step processes without missing steps

• Organized, reliable, and consistent

• Good written English

• Reliable internet connection

• Ecommerce or Shopify experience is a plus



GROWTH IN THIS ROLE:

As you get comfortable, you will be trained to help review designs for quality before they go into production (such as spotting weak or unsupported areas).



You are NOT expected to know this immediately — we will train you step by step.



GROWTH OPPORTUNITY:

This role has the potential for increased hours and expanded responsibilities over time as the business grows.



High-performing team members may also be considered for promotions into larger roles with more responsibility.



We are looking for someone long-term who wants to grow with the company.



TEAM & CULTURE:

We are a young, fast-growing business and are looking for someone who wants a long-term position, not just short-term work.



We value team members who are motivated, responsive, and eager to learn and grow with the company over time.



We are not looking for someone juggling many clients — we prefer someone who can focus and grow within one team.



COMPENSATION:

• $5 per hour

• 4 hours per day guaranteed



In the beginning, the workload may take around 2–3 hours per day, but you will still be paid for the full 4 hours.



As the business grows, the workload will increase and will fully utilize the 4 hours.



• Paid weekly

• Long-term role with growth opportunity



IMPORTANT:

We value reliability and communication. During hiring, we prioritize candidates who respond quickly and clearly.



To confirm you read this job post, please start your application with the word "Steel".



Applications without this will not be considered.



Bonus:

If you have worked with Shopify orders before, briefly explain your process when a new order comes in.

- Be trained by the best in Workers Compensation defense - we’ll invest in training you

- Be empowered to make decisions

- Have the authority to direct your own path

- Work without being micromanaged

- Work from home

- Emphasis on relative work-life balance in the legal field

- Competitive salary



At Pacific Admin Solutions, we value each of our tea
mbers, and together, we join forces with a top notch law firm in implementing their legal strategies, resulting in out-of-the-box thinking and effective defense of Workers’ Compensation claims.



We work with dedicated and experienced Workers’ Compensation Defense attorneys who work as a cohesive unit to provide unrivaled representation for their clients. The law firm we work with defends every claim with the highest standards.



Our goal is to help our partner attorneys dedicate themselves to their clients’ defense and defend every claim with a unique approach and plan of action.



And to do that, we collaborate with clients to create and implement case specific strategies tailored to reaching favorable results. Come join us in making a difference.



This is a role that will require decision-making, responsibility and independence in your work. We need you to handle the review of attorney-client status letters, involving everything from checking grammar/punctuation of transcribed letters, to then drafting additional letters based on context, to ensuring that all other necessary tasks are being worked on. We want you to play a huge role in things moving forward.



This is an exciting long-term opportunity for you.



As you grow, the position can grow with you, based on your skills and interests.



Whichever direction your passion and talent take you, we’ll offer you training and mentorship so that you really can live up to your potential.



So, are you ready?



JOB OVERVIEW

Main Responsibilities:

- Reading and comprehending case status reports that have been dictated by an attorney, ensuring that there are no grammar and punctuation mistakes, and cross-referencing to make sure that all names are spelled correctly.

- Following attorney-transcribed instructions to draft any additional letters to different parties and making sure any action items are tasked and being worked on by others.

- Will need to be able to sift through large client files with speed and accuracy.

- Familiarity with law-related parties and terms is not required, but will most certainly help.

- Prioritizing urgent tasks and being able to triage work according to what should be completed first



We’re looking for someone who produces a high level of work with meticulous attention to detail. You will be given a certain amount of independence and responsibility to figure out what you need to do and to do it correctly.



Here are some specific examples of the tasks you’ll be handling:

- Going through a case status report - verbalized by an attorney into a 3rd party app/service, that has been transcribed by that service - to ensure that there are no spelling or grammatical mistakes, and that all party names are spelled correctly. The transcription service is fairly accurate, but there will be mistakes and it is your job to find them and clean up the mess.

- In addition to scanning the status report/letter for standard mistakes, you’ll also need to grasp the context of letter to understand what’s going on at a high level.

- You are going to be asked to prepare further correspondence with the various parties of the cases. These will vary from requesting medicals, prescriptions or MRIs from physicians to inquiring with the adverse attorney or co-party about settlement possibilities, to other legal process follow-ups.



Thanks for reading this far! To apply, as the title/header of your application response, please write "Pacific Admin Solutions’ Rockstar”. Also please send me a video introducing yourself (can be a link to a loom or YT vid) briefly discussing the hardest challenge you have faced and how you overcame it (custom, non-template, non-generic video please). Make the video under 2 minutes.



DESCRIPTION OF IDEAL CANDIDATE

- Takes Pride in Mastering the Details

You have an eye for detail. When you say your work is correct, it’s correct. You cross all of your T’s and dot all of your I’s. You will double (or even triple) check your work to make sure that you did it the correct way.



- Listens To Instructions

Double checking your work doesn’t mean anything if you didn’t follow the correct instructions in the first place. You will listen (or read) to the instructions given to you, making sure that you understand the task at hand. And if the task doesn’t make sense to you, you won’t be afraid to ask to make sure you do the job correctly.



- Does the Job Right

You find a sense of accomplishment in ensuring that you do the job correctly, as you were instructed to do. If you have a question during the onboarding or learning process, you aren’t afraid to ask in order to make sure you do the job right.



- Works Well Independently and on a Team

You are able to take on a task when you aren’t given specific instructions for every single detail involved, and can use a higher level understanding to assume responsibility for the work and make proper decisions. At the same time, you are able to work seamlessly with others - you can exercise empathy and teach a teammate that needs help, and show a growth mindset that is receptive to feedback when learning from others.



- Gets Stuff Done

You take charge and get stuff done! You know you can ask for feedback and resources, but you don’t make excuses. You have a passion and an internal drive to make things happen!



- Self Starter

You’re self-motivated, happy working on your own with minimal guidance. You don’t understand why some people just sit there when they haven't been told what to do.



- Thrives With Hard Work

Some people shrink when given a big project. Not you - you say “bring it on!” It’s just one more opportunity to prove what you’re capable of.



- Solves Problems

Reason, logic, and process is your zone of genius. Your friends are often amazed at how you find solutions so quickly, often before they even realize there's a problem.



- Growth-minded

You’re always learning, always growing. If you weren’t, life would be stale and boring.



- Takes Ownership

You love to be in charge, and you want more responsibility. You’re confident to say, “let me handle this.”



- You Do What You Say

If you say you’ll do something, you’ll do it. And if you can’t do it, you’ll say you can’t. (Why do some people make promises they can’t keep?!)



- Pleasure To Work With

Life’s too short to work 
 looking for someone with a positive attitude who knows how to communicate with his/her coworkers.



COMPANY CULTURE

We're transparent; ask if something isn't clear, feel comfortable to express your difference of opinion. We work hard, we have fun, we believe in a fair work-life balance in the legal field.



PAY

Based on ability and experience, ranging from around 35,000-45,000 PHP per month, for this full-time role (depending on a combination of skills, ability and experience). If you believe you have more to offer, tell us what you’d like and why you’re able to deliver more value.



CANDIDATE REQUIREMENTS

- Honest

- Reliable

- Hard working

- Attention to Detail

- Fluent in English (written and spoken), near native speaker

- Type 50 WPM

- Own computer capable of handling data entry apps such as adobe reader and excel

- Strong/reliable internet connection

- Good character - *will have to be willing to conduct a background check. This role involves work in the legal field, so you will be dealing with sensitive client and medical provider files.



Preferred:

- Microsoft Office and/or Adobe PDF experience



ADDITIONAL DETAILS

*REQUIRED:

- Internet: Fast, stable connection.

- Computer with sufficient specs to be able to handle adobe and microsoft suites (excel, word)

- Background check (no previous criminal background)



Work Environment: Be able to jump on a zoom video call with coworkers during business hours. Need to be able to respond to work-related chat/email communications in a timely manner.



WORKING HOURS

Full time position (40 hours/week): Working hours are ~Monday - Friday, 8AM - 4PM PHT time (with 2-3 hours on Saturday/Sunday mornings). For this position in particular, the days worked are as noted above, though there will be a lit of bit of flexibility in the tangential hours. PLEASE NOTE that your work days and hours may vary, especially during the initial training period, and/or when having to communicate/coordinate with other tea
mbers/clients.



HOW TO APPLY

Respond to the job post with your resume and any requested information.



We look forward to working with you!



Sean Rose

Pacific Admin Solutions

title: SEO Specialist
description:
We are looking for a high-level Local SEO expert who specializes in Google Business Profile growth and Google Maps rankings.



This role is for someone who understands how to dominate local search, build authority backlinks, and turn Google Maps visibility into real leads and revenue.



We are not looking for a general SEO assistant.

We are looking for someone who has already ranked businesses in the top 3 of Google Maps and can repeat the process consistently.



Your Mission

Increase Google Maps rankings, calls, and leads by building authority, optimizing Google Business Profiles, and executing strong local SEO strategies.



Key Responsibilities

Google Business Profile Growth

Optimize Google Business Profiles for higher Google Maps rankings



Improve local relevance, proximity, and authority signals



Manage GMB posts, services, products, Q&A, and updates



Implement review acquisition strategies



Local SEO Strategy

Identify high-intent local keywords



Optimize location pages and local landing pages



Conduct local competitor analysis



Improve Google Maps visibility



Backlink & Authority Building

Build high-quality local backlinks



Create citation and directory strategies



Develop authority signals for local ranking



Execute link outreach campaigns



Performance Tracking

Track Google Maps rankings



Monitor calls, leads, and traffic



Deliver weekly performance reports



Requirements

You must have real, proven results with Local SEO.



Proven success ranking businesses in Google Maps Top 3



Strong experience with Google Business Profile optimization



Expertise in local backlinks and citations



Deep understanding of Local SEO ranking factors



Experience with tools like Ahrefs, SEMrush, BrightLocal, Whitespark, SurferSEO



Ability to analyze competitors and reverse engineer rankings



Bonus Experience

Home services industries (roofing, remodeling, construction)



Multi-location SEO strategies



Reputation management and review generation



Schema and local structured data



Success Metrics

Your performance will be measured by:



Google Maps ranking improvements



Increase in calls and leads



Growth in domain authority and backlinks



Organic traffic growth



To Apply

Please include:



Google Business Profiles you ranked



Keywords where you achieved Top 3 Google Maps rankings



Your backlink strategy for local SEO



Tools you use for Local SEO growth



Respond with:



What is the capital of California?



Send a video responding to all these questions:



1. How would you increase the visibility and authority of our Google Business Profile?

Please explain the specific actions you would take to grow and improve our GMB page.



2. Describe the exact steps you would take to rank a Google Business Profile in the Top 3 of Google Maps in a competitive market.



3. Explain your backlink strategy for Local SEO.

What types of backlinks do you build to improve Google Maps rankings?



4. If a competitor is ranking higher on Google Maps, how would you analyze and reverse engineer their strategy?



5. Do you have access to backlinks?





Only respond to these questions in a video format.

title: Marketing Manager-  SEO, ADS, SMM
description:
Looking for a Marketing Manager / Digital Marketing Director

We’re seeking a results-driven Marketing Manager / Digital Marketing Director with deep expertise across the entire digital marketing landscape. This role demands both strategic leadership and hands-on execution — the ideal candidate knows how to build high-performing digital strategies and turn them into measurable growth.



If you live and breathe data-driven marketing, online lead generation, and digital scale, you’ll thrive in this role.



Key Responsibilities

Digital Marketing Strategy



Develop and execute a comprehensive digital marketing plan focused on lead generation, brand growth, and ROI.



Integrate all marketing channels — SEO, paid ads, content, and automation — into one cohesive growth system.



SEO Leadership



Lead all aspects of SEO, including on-page, local, and technical SEO, plus link-building strategies that enhance domain authority and local visibility.



Continuously audit, test, and improve website performance and search rankings.



Paid Advertising (Google & Meta)



Build, manage, and optimize Google Ads and
/Meta Ads campaigns targeting high-quality traffic and strong conversion rates.



Develop creative ad strategies that cut through noise and deliver measurable revenue outcomes.



Content Marketing & Brand Storytelling



Oversee creation of high-impact blogs, landing pages, and digital content that strengthen brand authority and drive conversions.



Guide messaging strategy to ensure every piece of content supports the buyer journey.



Analytics, Optimization & Reporting



Use analytics tools (Google Analytics, Search Console, Ads Manager, etc.) to track campaign performance and uncover growth opportunities.



Translate data insights into actionable marketing improvements.



Marketing Systems & Funnel Optimization



Build and refine digital funnels, CRM systems, and conversion tracking.



Identify bottlenecks and continuously test new strategies to lower acquisition costs and improve lead quality.



Competitive & Market Intelligence



Monitor competitors’ digital strategies and implement data-backed tactics to outperform them across all digital platforms.



What We’re Looking For

Proven experience leading SEO, Google Ads, and Meta Ads campaigns.



Expertise in Local SEO and Google Business Profile optimization.



Strong understanding of marketing funnels, digital conversions, and growth mechanics.



Analytical mindset with the ability to turn performance data into strategic decisions.



Excellent written communication — especially for ad copy, landing pages, and content marketing.



Familiarity with marketing tools, including Google Analytics, Search Console, Ads, SEMrush, Ahrefs, or similar.



A self-starter passionate about testing, innovating, and scaling digital results.



The Ideal Candidate

You think like a marketer, strategist, and growth architect. You understand customer psychology, know how to build digital authority, and thrive on measurable performance.

You are always testing, improving, and scaling — looking for that next creative way to generate more leads at lower cost.



Screening Questions (Required)

Please answer these questions in detail:



If a company’s Google Business Profile isn’t ranking in the Top 3 of Google Maps, what exact steps would you take to improve it?



Describe a Google Ads campaign you personally managed. What was the monthly budget, cost per lead, and what optimizations did you make?



Explain your backlink strategy for Local SEO. What types of backlinks would you build to improve rankings?



If a competitor is ranking higher on Google Maps, how would you analyze and reverse-engineer their strategy?



Describe your process for writing SEO blog content that truly ranks on Google.



What are the three most important metrics you track when managing Google Ads campaigns?



Share one real example of a business you helped grow through digital marketing — what actions did you take, and what results did you achieve?

title: Customer Reactivation and Revenue Closer
description:
CUSTOMER REACTIVATION & REVENUE CLOSER (REMOTE)

READ THIS FIRST

We are not hiring average.

We are hiring someone who can:

Communicate clearly

Execute consistently

Owner the result

This is NOT cold calling.

You are reactivating past customers who already know us.

If you can’t turn warm conversations into revenue, don’t apply.



THE ROLE

You will call past customers and reactivate them with a simple offer.

That’s it.

No long sales cycles.

No chasing cold leads.

No complicated products.

You:

• Call past clients 

• Present one offer 

• Close on the call 

• Collect payment 

• Move on 



WHAT YOU’LL DO DAILY

• Make 200+ calls/day to past customers 

• Have 25+ real conversations 

• Close 5–10+ deals/day 

• Send payment links via SMS/email 

• Confirm payment on the call 

• Update CRM 

• Send follow-up
 after close 

• Ask for 2–3 referrals every time 



WHAT MAKES THIS DIFFERENT

You are NOT calling strangers.

You are calling:

People who have already done business with us

That means:

• Higher trust 

• Faster conversations 

• Easier closes 

If you’re good, this becomes predictable income quickly.



HOW WE SELL

We sell ONE offer.

No options.

No confusion.

No overexplaining.

You say:

“Do you want me to reserve that for you?”

Then you send the link.



CORE VALUES (HOW YOU OPERATE)



1. CLEAR COMMUNICATION

You say what needs to be said simply and directly.

• No rambling 

• No confusion 

• No filler 

If they don’t understand, that’s on you.



2. RELENTLESS CONSISTENCY

You execute every day.

• 200 calls = 200 calls 

• No drop-off 

• No excuses 

Consistency wins here.



3. CONFIDENT OWNERSHIP

You own your results.

• Calls 

• Conversations 

• Revenue 

No blaming:

• leads 

• timing 

• script 

You adjust and improve.



TOOLS YOU’LL USE

• Dialer ? GoHighLevel 

• CRM ? HubSpot 

• Payments ? Stripe 

• SMS +
 

You must move fast between all of them.



COMPENSATION

• Hourly: $6–$10/hr

o $10–$15 per sale 

o Bi-weekly bonuses 



EXAMPLE

10 sales/day:

$100–$150 bonus/day

Plus hourly



SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

• You close within your first few days 

• You confidently ask for payment 

• You move quickly from call to call 

• You improve daily 



YOU WILL FAIL IF

• You hesitate 

• You talk too much 

• You avoid asking for the sale 

• You get discouraged 



CALL PROCESS

1. Open 

2. Present offer 

3. Ask for sale 

4. Send payment link 

5. Confirm payment 

6. Log in CRM 

7. Ask for referrals 

8. Thank you
 and intro



FINAL WORD

This role rewards:

Clear communication

Relentless consistency

Confident ownership

You are working a warm list with a proven offer.

If you can close:  You will make money here

If you can’t: You won’t last



Put this in your subject: Closer Application – I Close on the First Call

I would like a 60 second video on why you and why now!

Other

title: Outbound Enrollment Closer – Career Certification Programs (Remote, EST)
description:
Outbound Enrollment Closer – Career Certification Programs (Remote, EST)



This is not customer service.

This is not inbound order-taking.



This is a phone-driven, outbound enrollment closer role for professionals who know how to control a consult, qualify fast, overcome objections, and close.



We are a U.S.-based online career school enrolling students into IT and Project Management certification programs (CompTIA, PMP, and related tracks). We provide warm leads. You bring the discipline, urgency, and closing skill.



What You’ll Be Doing (Daily Reality)



Make high-volume outbound calls to prospective students



Run structured, consultative enrollment calls (needs analysis ? recommendation ? close)



Control the call, set next steps, and ask for the enrollment



Handle objections around price, time, confidence, and urgency



Follow up relentlessly until the prospect converts or disqualifies



Log activity and outcomes accurately in CRM



Hit daily activity targets and monthly enrollment goals



Improve close rates through coaching, scripts, and call reviews



If you don’t like calling, this role will break you.



Who This Role Is For



Proven outbound / inside sales closers



Comfortable dialing daily and living on the phone



Strong command of English with clear U.S. cultural fluency



Confident asking direct questions and pushing for decisions



Competitive, coachable, metrics-driven



Organized and accountable without supervision



Experience selling education, training, coaching, or professional services is a major plus



Familiarity with certifications (CompTIA, PMP) helps—but closing ability matters more



Bachelor’s degree required



Who Should NOT Apply



Anyone who needs inbound-only leads



Anyone uncomfortable asking for money



Anyone who avoids objection handling



Anyone who wants a “soft” admissions role



Anyone who disappears when targets tighten



Schedule



Monday–Friday



10:00 AM – 6:30 PM EST



Compensation



Base pay: $1,200–$1,600 USD per month



Performance-based advancement and leadership path



Long-term role with stability and growth for performers



(Top performers move fast. Average performers don’t last.)



What You Get



Warm leads with real intent



Clear scripts, frameworks, and coaching



No gimmicks, no fake promises—just real programs that work



A sales culture that rewards results



Bottom Line



If you can:



Control a sales conversation



Build urgency without lying



Close consistently



Take coaching and improve



You will win here.



If not—this role will expose you quickly.

title: Credit Specialist
description:
Job Description

The Credit Specialist will be responsible for reviewing, analyzing, and managing credit reports, assisting clients with credit repair strategies, and ensuring accurate documentation. You will also communicate with credit bureaus, creditors, and clients to resolve disputes, track progress, and provide regular updates.

This role requires strong attention to detail, analytical skills, and discretion with sensitive financial information.

Key Responsibilities

    •    Review client credit reports for errors, inaccuracies, and opportunities for improvement.

mbers to improve internal systems and processes.

Work Schedule

How to Apply
